Master bill of lading number issued for the cargo transported on this voyage. Primary shipping document and title of goods.
reference: Bill of Lading, International Maritime Standards

description: Total bunker fuel consumed during the voyage, expressed in metric tons. Includes all fuel types (HFO, MGO, VLSFO, etc.).
Total cost of bunker fuel consumed during the voyage. Expressed in freight currency. Major component of voyage costs.
Total fees paid for canal transits (e.g., Suez Canal, Panama Canal) during the voyage. Expressed in freight currency.
Total quantity of cargo loaded for this voyage. Unit of measure is specified in cargo_quantity_uom.
Unit of measure for cargo quantity. BBL = Barrels, MT = Metric Tons, M3 = Cubic Meters, MMBTU = Million British Thermal Units, GAL = Gallons.
reference: API Standards, International System of Units

description: Reference number or identifier of the charter party agreement governing this voyage. Links to contract management system.

Type of charter party agreement governing this voyage. Voyage charter is for a single voyage, time charter is for a period, bareboat charter transfers operational control, contract of affreightment is for multiple voyages, and spot is an ad-hoc arrangement.
Timestamp when the voyage officially commenced, typically when the vessel departed the first load port or when loading operations began.
Timestamp when the voyage was completed, typically when discharge operations finished at the final discharge port and the vessel was released.
Timestamp when this voyage record was first created in the system.
Total demurrage amount payable by the charterer, calculated from demurrage hours and rate. Expressed in freight currency.
Total amount claimed in the demurrage claim, which may differ from the calculated demurrage_amount due to negotiations or disputes. Expressed in freight currency.
Unique reference number for the demurrage claim submitted by the shipowner to the charterer.
Current status of the demurrage claim in the settlement process. Tracks progression from draft through to final settlement or withdrawal.
Description of the reason for dispute if the demurrage claim is contested by the charterer. Includes details of disagreements on laytime calculations, exceptions, or force majeure events.
Total demurrage hours incurred when laytime used exceeds laytime allowed. Demurrage is a penalty paid by the charterer for exceeding allowed time.
Agreed daily rate for demurrage charges, expressed in the freight currency. Used to calculate total demurrage amount.
Date when the demurrage claim was finally settled and payment was made or agreed.
Total despatch amount payable by the shipowner to the charterer, calculated from despatch hours and rate. Expressed in freight currency.
Total despatch hours earned when laytime used is less than laytime allowed. Despatch is a bonus paid by the shipowner to the charterer for saving time.
Agreed daily rate for despatch payments, typically half the demurrage rate. Expressed in the freight currency.
Date when the charter party agreement was fixed and agreed between the charterer and shipowner.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the freight rate and voyage financial amounts.
The agreed freight rate for the voyage. Interpretation depends on freight_rate_basis (e.g., Worldscale points, lump sum amount, or per-unit rate).
Basis on which freight is calculated. Worldscale is a standard tanker rate index, lump sum is a fixed amount for the voyage, per-unit rates are based on cargo volume or weight.
The legal jurisdiction and law governing the charter party agreement (e.g., English Law, New York Law).
Timestamp when this voyage record was last updated in the system.
End date of the laycan window. If the vessel does not arrive by this date, the charterer may have the right to cancel the charter.
Start date of the laycan (laydays and canceling) window. The earliest date the vessel is expected to arrive at the load port.
Terms and conditions for freight payment, including timing, method, and any advance or retention clauses.
Total port charges incurred across all port calls during the voyage, including port dues, pilotage, tugs, and agency fees. Expressed in freight currency.

Net profit or loss for the voyage, calculated as freight revenue minus voyage costs (bunker, port charges, canal fees, demurrage/despatch net). Expressed in freight currency. Business-confidential financial metric.
Total laytime (loading and discharge time) allowed under the charter party terms, expressed in hours. Laytime is the time allowed for cargo operations.
Total laytime actually used across all load and discharge ports, expressed in hours. Calculated from port call records.
Business identifier for the voyage, typically assigned by the commercial or operations team. Used for external communication and documentation.
Current operational status of the voyage. Tracks the voyage lifecycle from planning through completion or cancellation.

description: Temperature of produced gas at measurement point in degrees Fahrenheit. Used for volume correction to standard conditions.
reference: API MPMS Chapter 14 - Natural Gas Fluids Measurement

description: Gas-oil ratio expressed as cubic feet of gas produced per barrel of oil (SCF/BBL). Key reservoir performance indicator.

business_glossary_term: Gross Gas Volume (Thousand Cubic Feet per Day - MCFD)
description: Total gross natural gas production volume in thousand cubic feet (MCF) for the production date before any deductions. Measured at standard conditions (60°F, 14.73 psia).
reference: API MPMS Chapter 14 - Natural Gas Fluids Measurement

business_glossary_term: Gross Oil Volume (Barrels of Oil Per Day - BOPD)
description: Total gross oil production volume in barrels for the production date before any deductions for royalties, working interest, or shrinkage. Measured at standard conditions (60°F, 14.7 psia).
reference: API MPMS Chapter 12 - Calculation of Petroleum Quantities

business_glossary_term: Gross Water Volume (Barrels of Water Per Day - BWPD)
description: Total gross water production volume in barrels for the production date. Includes produced formation water and any injected water returned to surface.
reference: API RP 45 - Recommended Practice for Analysis of Oilfield Waters

business_glossary_term: Hydrogen Sulfide (H2S) Content (Parts Per Million - PPM)
description: Hydrogen sulfide concentration in produced gas measured in parts per million. Critical for safety and gas quality specifications.
reference: API RP 55 - Recommended Practice for Oil and Gas Producing and Gas Processing Plant Operations Involving Hydrogen Sulfide

business_glossary_term: Net Gas Volume (Thousand Cubic Feet - MCF)
description: Net natural gas production volume in thousand cubic feet after applying working interest, net revenue interest, and any applicable deductions. Represents the company's entitlement share.
Net oil production volume in barrels after applying working interest, net revenue interest, and any applicable deductions. Represents the company's entitlement share.
Natural gas liquids production volume in barrels extracted from the gas stream. Includes ethane, propane, butane, and natural gasoline.
reference: API MPMS Chapter 14 - Natural Gas Fluids Measurement

business_glossary_term: Oil Gravity (American Petroleum Institute - API Degrees)
description: API gravity of produced crude oil. Measure of oil density relative to water. Higher API gravity indicates lighter, more valuable crude.
reference: API MPMS Chapter 11.1 - Volume Correction Factors

description: Temperature of produced oil at measurement point in degrees Fahrenheit. Used for volume correction to standard conditions.
reference: API MPMS Chapter 11 - Physical Properties Data

description: Calendar date for which production volumes are reported. Represents the business event date of production activity.

Operational status of the well on the production date. Indicates whether the well was actively producing, shut-in for maintenance, down due to equipment failure, undergoing testing, flowing naturally, or on artificial lift.

business_glossary_term: Pump Stroke Rate (Strokes Per Minute - SPM)
description: Operating stroke rate of rod pump in strokes per minute. Key parameter for artificial lift optimization.
reference: API RP 11L - Recommended Practice for Design Calculations for Sucker Rod Pumping Systems

description: Timestamp when this production record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage and compliance.
Timestamp when this production record was last modified. Audit trail for data lineage and compliance.
Reference number of the oil run ticket or sales ticket associated with this production. Links production to custody transfer and sales records.

business_glossary_term: Separator Pressure (Pounds per Square Inch - PSI)
description: Operating pressure of the production separator in pounds per square inch. Affects gas-liquid separation efficiency and volume measurement.
reference: API Spec 12J - Specification for Oil and Gas Separators

business_glossary_term: Tubing Pressure (Pounds per Square Inch - PSI)
description: Flowing tubing pressure measured at the wellhead in pounds per square inch. Key indicator of well performance and reservoir pressure.
reference: API RP 11G - Recommended Practice for Installation and Locking of Sucker Rods

description: Number of hours the well was actively producing during the 24-hour production period. Used to calculate production rates and identify downtime.
Percentage of water in the total liquid production (water + oil). Calculated as (water volume / (water volume + oil volume)) * 100.
reference: API RP 44 - Recommended Practice for Sampling Petroleum Reservoir Fluids

description: Water-oil ratio expressed as barrels of water produced per barrel of oil. Indicates reservoir water breakthrough and production efficiency.
Unique identifier for the meter station. Primary key for the meter station master record.

County or parish jurisdiction where the meter station is located.

Date when the meter station was taken out of service and decommissioned.
Maximum rated throughput capacity of the meter station as designed and certified by the manufacturer.

Date when the meter station was most recently calibrated and certified for accuracy.
Date when the meter station was last proven using a certified prover system to establish meter factor.
Geographic latitude coordinate of the meter station location in decimal degrees.
Geographic longitude coordinate of the meter station location in decimal degrees.
Accuracy classification of the meter station per API MPMS standards, indicating the precision level for fiscal or allocation purposes.
Type of fluid being measured at this meter station.
Quantified measurement uncertainty expressed as a percentage, representing the potential variance in measured values under normal operating conditions.

Technology type of the primary metering device installed at the station.

Current operational state of the meter station in its lifecycle.
Percentage ownership interest of the operator in this meter station, relevant for joint venture operations.
OSIsoft PI System tag identifier for real-time data integration and historian storage of meter station measurements.
Method used to prove the meter and establish the meter factor, per API MPMS Chapter 4 standards.
Timestamp when this meter station record was first created in the system.
Timestamp when this meter station record was most recently updated in the system.
Indicates whether measurements from this meter station must be included in regulatory production reports to BSEE, state agencies, or other governing bodies.

business_glossary_term: Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) Integration Flag
description: Indicates whether this meter station is integrated with SCADA systems for real-time monitoring and data acquisition.
reference: API MPMS Chapter 21 - Flow Measurement Using Electronic Metering Systems

description: State or province jurisdiction where the meter station is located, relevant for regulatory reporting.
Unique alphanumeric code assigned to the meter station for identification in operational systems and regulatory filings.
Business name or designation of the meter station for operational reference and reporting.

Classification of the meter station based on its primary business function: fiscal (revenue measurement), allocation (production distribution), custody transfer (ownership change point), check (verification), proving (calibration), or test (well testing).

Method used to allocate production volumes to the well or facility. Indicates whether volumes are directly measured or allocated from facility totals.

reference: API Bulletin D12A - API Well Number Standard

description: Total production expressed in barrels of oil equivalent (BOE), converting gas and NGL volumes to oil-equivalent barrels using standard conversion factors (typically 6 MCF = 1 BOE).

business_glossary_term: Bureau of Safety and Environmental Enforcement (BSEE) Submission Reference
description: Reference number or confirmation code assigned by BSEE upon successful submission of the monthly production report for offshore operations.
Date on which the monthly production record was certified and approved for regulatory submission.
reference: SOX Internal Controls for Production Reporting

description: Status of the monthly production record in the certification and regulatory submission workflow. Indicates whether volumes have been validated and submitted.
Name or identifier of the production engineer or authorized personnel who certified the monthly production volumes as accurate and complete.
reference: SOX Internal Controls for Production Reporting

description: Total volume of condensate produced during the month, measured in barrels (BBL). Condensate is liquid hydrocarbon recovered from gas wells.
reference: State Oil and Gas Commission Reporting Standards

description: Source system or method from which the monthly production data was extracted. Indicates data lineage and quality assurance level.
Total hours of non-productive time (NPT) during the month due to equipment failure, maintenance, or operational issues.

business_glossary_term: Flared Gas Volume in Thousand Cubic Feet (MCF)
description: Volume of natural gas flared during the month due to operational constraints or safety requirements, measured in thousand cubic feet (MCF).

business_glossary_term: Fuel Gas Volume in Thousand Cubic Feet (MCF)
description: Volume of natural gas consumed as fuel for lease operations and equipment during the month, measured in thousand cubic feet (MCF).

business_glossary_term: Gas Thousand Cubic Feet per Day (MCFD) Average
description: Average daily gas production rate for the month, calculated as total gas volume divided by the number of production days. Expressed in MCFD.

business_glossary_term: Gas Volume in Thousand Cubic Feet (MCF)
description: Total volume of natural gas produced during the month, measured in thousand cubic feet (MCF). This is the certified volume submitted to regulatory agencies.
Gas-oil ratio for the month, calculated as the ratio of gas volume (MCF) to oil volume (BBL). Indicates reservoir performance and production characteristics.
Percentage of net revenue interest after deducting royalties and overriding royalty interests. Represents the operators share of production revenue.

business_glossary_term: Natural Gas Liquids (NGL) Volume in Barrels (BBL)
description: Total volume of natural gas liquids (NGL) extracted during the month, measured in barrels (BBL). Includes ethane, propane, butane, and natural gasoline.

business_glossary_term: Oil Barrels of Oil Per Day (BOPD) Average
description: Average daily oil production rate for the month, calculated as total oil volume divided by the number of production days. Expressed in BOPD.
Total volume of crude oil produced during the month, measured in barrels (BBL). This is the certified volume submitted to regulatory agencies.
Number of days the well or facility was actively producing during the month. Used to calculate average daily rates and uptime.
The calendar month for which production volumes are reported, typically the first day of the month (YYYY-MM-01 format).
Current operational status of the well or facility during the production month. Indicates whether the asset was actively producing or offline.
Timestamp when the monthly production record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Timestamp when the monthly production record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
Date on which the certified monthly production volumes were submitted to the regulatory agency (BSEE, state oil and gas commission).
Free-text field for additional comments, explanations, or notes regarding the monthly production record, including operational issues or data quality concerns.
Regulatory jurisdiction to which the monthly production report is submitted. Determines the applicable reporting requirements and agency.

business_glossary_term: Reservoir Pressure in Pounds per Square Inch (PSI)
description: Average reservoir pressure during the production month, measured in pounds per square inch (PSI). Indicates reservoir energy and depletion status.

business_glossary_term: Royalty Volume in Barrel of Oil Equivalent (BOE)
description: Total production volume attributable to royalty interests, expressed in barrels of oil equivalent (BOE). Used for royalty payment calculation.

business_glossary_term: Sales Gas Volume in Thousand Cubic Feet (MCF)
description: Volume of natural gas sold or delivered to market during the month, measured in thousand cubic feet (MCF). Excludes flared, vented, and fuel gas.

business_glossary_term: Vented Gas Volume in Thousand Cubic Feet (MCF)
description: Volume of natural gas vented during the month, measured in thousand cubic feet (MCF). Reported for environmental compliance and emissions tracking.
Total volume of produced water during the month, measured in barrels (BBL). Includes formation water and injected water produced back.

business_glossary_term: Wellhead Pressure in Pounds per Square Inch (PSI)
description: Average wellhead flowing pressure during the production month, measured in pounds per square inch (PSI). Used for production optimization and decline analysis.
reference: API RP 11AR - Recommended Practice for Care and Use of Subsurface Pumps

description: Water-oil ratio for the month, calculated as the ratio of water volume (BBL) to oil volume (BBL). Indicates water breakthrough and reservoir depletion.
Percentage of working interest held by the operator in the well or facility for the production month. Used for revenue and cost allocation.
Unique identifier for the production allocation record. Primary key for the production allocation transaction.

Reference to the well test record that drives the allocation factors for test-ratio allocation method. Links to well test analysis data.

The methodology used to distribute facility-level production back to individual wells. Test-ratio uses well test results, meter-based uses individual well meters, volumetric proportional uses reservoir properties.

The date and time when this allocation was approved and moved to final status. Critical for audit trail and regulatory compliance.

business_glossary_term: Barrel of Oil Equivalent (BOE) Conversion Factor
description: The conversion factor used to convert gas volumes to oil equivalent barrels. Typically 6.0 MCF equals 1 BOE, but may vary by operator or regulatory requirement.

description: The total gas volume measured at the facility level before allocation to individual wells. Used as the basis for allocation calculations and variance reconciliation.

business_glossary_term: Facility Metered Oil Volume in Barrels (BBL)
description: The total oil volume measured at the facility level before allocation to individual wells. Used as the basis for allocation calculations and variance reconciliation.

description: The calculated proportion or ratio used to allocate facility production to this well. Expressed as a decimal fraction (e.g., 0.235 represents 23.5% of facility production).

business_glossary_term: Gas Allocation Variance in Thousand Cubic Feet (MCF)
description: The difference between facility metered gas volume and the sum of all allocated gas volumes to wells. Represents unallocated or over-allocated volumes requiring reconciliation.
The ratio of gas production to oil production, expressed as cubic feet of gas per barrel of oil. Key reservoir performance indicator used for decline curve analysis and reservoir management.

business_glossary_term: Gross Gas Volume in Thousand Cubic Feet (MCF)
description: Total allocated natural gas production volume for the well before interest deductions, measured in thousand cubic feet. Represents 100% working interest volume.

business_glossary_term: Gross Natural Gas Liquids (NGL) Volume in Barrels (BBL)
description: Total allocated natural gas liquids production volume for the well before interest deductions, measured in barrels. Includes condensate, propane, butane, and natural gasoline.

description: Total allocated oil production volume for the well before interest deductions, measured in barrels. Represents 100% working interest volume.

description: Total allocated water production volume for the well before interest deductions, measured in barrels. Used for water-oil ratio (WOR) analysis and disposal tracking.
reference: API RP 45 - Recommended Practice for Analysis of Oilfield Waters

description: The date when this allocation was posted to the Joint Interest Billing system for partner invoicing. Links production volumes to revenue distribution and partner settlements.
The date and time when this allocation record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit trail. Updated whenever any field in the record changes.

business_glossary_term: Net Gas Volume in Thousand Cubic Feet (MCF)
description: Net allocated gas production volume after applying working interest decimal. Calculated as gross_gas_volume_mcf multiplied by working_interest_decimal. Used for partner revenue distribution.

business_glossary_term: Net Natural Gas Liquids (NGL) Volume in Barrels (BBL)
description: Net allocated NGL production volume after applying working interest decimal. Calculated as gross_ngl_volume_bbl multiplied by working_interest_decimal. Used for partner revenue distribution.
Net allocated oil production volume after applying working interest decimal. Calculated as gross_oil_volume_bbl multiplied by working_interest_decimal. Used for partner revenue distribution.

The ownership percentage representing the net revenue interest share after royalty and overriding royalty deductions. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.60 represents 60% NRI). Used for revenue distribution calculations.
The difference between facility metered oil volume and the sum of all allocated oil volumes to wells. Represents unallocated or over-allocated volumes requiring reconciliation.
The ending date of the production allocation period. Defines the time window for which volumes are allocated.
The beginning date of the production allocation period. Typically daily or monthly allocation cycles.
The calendar month for which production is allocated, expressed in YYYY-MM format. Used for monthly production reporting and trend analysis.
The date when allocation variances were reconciled and the allocation was finalized. Used for audit trail and compliance reporting.
Free-text explanation of variance causes and reconciliation actions taken. Documents meter calibration issues, well downtime, measurement errors, or allocation method adjustments.
Status of the variance reconciliation process. Indicates whether allocation variances have been investigated and resolved. Critical for audit trail and regulatory compliance.
Indicates whether this allocation record must be included in regulatory production reports to BSEE, state agencies, or SEC reserves disclosure. True if regulatory reporting is required.
The date and time when the allocation calculation was executed. Used for audit trail and to track allocation process timing.

The system of record that originated this allocation data. Avocet Production Operations for daily allocation, OSIsoft PI for real-time data, SCADA for facility meters, manual entry for adjustments, or third-party for operator-provided data.
Total production expressed in barrels of oil equivalent. Calculated by converting gas and NGL volumes to oil equivalent and summing with oil volumes. Used for standardized production reporting.
The ratio of water production to oil production, expressed as barrels of water per barrel of oil. Key indicator of reservoir water breakthrough and well performance decline.
The ownership percentage representing the working interest share for this allocation record. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.75 represents 75% WI). Used to calculate net volumes from gross volumes.
