description: Primary commercial channel through which product is sold or delivered to this account. Direct: bilateral contract. Broker: intermediary-facilitated. Exchange: commodity exchange platform. Pipeline: pipeline tariff-based delivery. Marine: tanker or vessel-based delivery. Retail: downstream retail network.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Distribution Channel; FERC Tariff Regulations

description: SAP SD sales organization code to which this account is assigned. Defines the legal entity responsible for the sale, the applicable pricing conditions, and the revenue booking entity for financial consolidation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Sales Organization Structure; IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition

description: Indicates whether the counterparty associated with this account has been screened against applicable sanctions lists (OFAC SDN, EU Consolidated List, UN Sanctions). True = screened and cleared. False = not yet screened or screening pending.
reference: OFAC Sanctions Regulations; EU Sanctions Compliance; FATF Guidelines

description: Date on which the account status was last changed (e.g., from active to suspended, or from pending to active). Critical for SOX audit compliance and temporal status tracking in the Silver layer.
reference: SOX Section 404 Audit Trail; SAP S/4HANA SD Change Management

description: Business reason code or description explaining the most recent account status change (e.g., credit limit breach, KYC expiry, counterparty request, sanctions flag, commercial closure). Supports audit trail and compliance reporting.
reference: SOX Section 404; SAP S/4HANA SD Change Management; OFAC Compliance

description: Tax classification assigned to this account in SAP FI/SD, determining applicable VAT, excise duty, and withholding tax treatment for transactions. Varies by counterparty jurisdiction and product type.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Tax Management; IFRS/GAAP Tax Accounting; EPA Excise Tax Regulations

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the account record in the enterprise data platform. Conforms to ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change data capture, incremental processing, and audit trail.
reference: SOX Section 404; ISO 8601; SAP S/4HANA Audit Log

description: Value Added Tax (VAT) or equivalent indirect tax registration number of the counterparty. Required for tax invoice compliance, cross-border transaction reporting, and VAT reclaim processing.
reference: IFRS/GAAP Tax Reporting; EU VAT Directive; SAP S/4HANA FI Tax

business_glossary_term: Volume Commitment in Barrels of Oil Per Day (BOPD)
description: Contracted minimum or nominated daily volume commitment for this account expressed in Barrels of Oil Per Day (BOPD). Applicable for term offtake accounts. Used for production planning, logistics scheduling, and take-or-pay obligation tracking.
reference: API Measurement Standards; FERC Gas Tariff Volume Commitments; SAP S/4HANA SD Contracts

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the individual contact person record within the Oil Gas customer domain. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins and relationship resolution.
reference: ISO/IEC 11179 Data Element Naming and Definition Standards

description: Reference to the parent counterparty or customer account (IOC, NOC, refiner, trader, industrial consumer) to which this contact belongs. Supports account hierarchy and relationship management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module — Business Partner Contact Assignment

description: Foreign key linking to customer.customer_counterparty. Business justification: Contacts can be associated directly with counterparties (not just through accounts). This supports scenarios where a contact represents the counterparty entity itself. The organization_name on contact

Maximum monetary value in US Dollars that this contact is authorized to approve for transactions, AFEs (Authorization for Expenditure), purchase orders, or contract amendments on behalf of their organization. Critical for COPAS AFE management and SOX financial controls.
reference: COPAS — Authorization for Expenditure (AFE) approval thresholds; SOX Section 302/404 financial authorization controls; SAP S/4HANA MM — Purchasing authorization limits

description: Name of the executive assistant or administrative contact who manages scheduling and correspondence on behalf of this contact. Used for executive-level relationship management with senior IOC, NOC, and industrial customer representatives.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD — Contact Person Assistant field; CRM relationship management best practices

description: Phone number for the executive assistant or administrative contact. Used to schedule meetings and route communications for senior counterparty representatives who delegate correspondence management.
reference: GDPR Article 4; ITU-T E.164; SAP S/4HANA SD — Assistant Phone field

description: Indicates the contractual and operational authority level of the contact: signatory (authorized to execute contracts/AFEs), approver (can approve transactions up to defined limits), operational (day-to-day operational decisions), informational (receives communications only). Critical for AFE authorization, JIB approvals, and contract execution workflows.
reference: COPAS — Authorization for Expenditure (AFE) signatory requirements; SOX Section 302/404 authorization controls; JOA counterparty authority definitions

description: Functional role classification of the contact in the context of the Oil Gas business relationship. Determines which communications, documents, and workflows are routed to this individual. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: commercial|technical|legal|hse_liaison|financial|executive|operational|procurement|regulatory — promote to reference product]
reference: API RP 100; JOA (Joint Operating Agreement) counterparty role definitions; SAP S/4HANA SD Contact Function

description: Current lifecycle status of the contact record. Active contacts are eligible for communications and workflow routing. Inactive or terminated contacts are retained for audit and historical reference but excluded from active routing. Supports KYC refresh and relationship management processes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA BP — Partner Status; GDPR Article 17 right to erasure vs. retention obligations; SOX audit trail requirements

description: Classification of the contact's relationship type to the account: primary (main point of contact), secondary (backup), emergency (24/7 incident response), billing (JIB and invoice recipient), technical (operations and engineering). Supports structured relationship management across IOC, NOC, and industrial customer organizations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD — Contact Person Function; COPAS JIB billing contact requirements

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code representing the country where the contact is primarily based or operates. Used for jurisdictional compliance, KYC screening, sanctions screening, and regulatory reporting.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; OFAC sanctions screening requirements; FATF KYC guidelines; SEC disclosure requirements

description: Timestamp when this contact record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides the audit trail creation anchor for SOX compliance and data governance.
reference: SOX Section 404 — audit trail requirements; ISO 8601; GDPR Article 30 records of processing activities

description: Organizational department or business unit of the contact within their counterparty organization (e.g., Trading, Procurement, Legal, HSE, Operations, Finance, Executive). Supports routing of commercial, technical, and legal communications.
reference: SAP S/4HANA BP — Department field; COPAS organizational classification

description: Direct dial telephone number for the contact in E.164 international format. Used for urgent operational communications, SIMOPS coordination, HSE incident notifications, and commercial negotiations.
reference: GDPR Article 4; ITU-T E.164 international telephone numbering plan; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.119 emergency contact requirements

description: Indicates whether this contact has requested to be excluded from non-essential communications. True = do not contact except for legally required or contractually obligated communications. Supports GDPR opt-out and communication preference compliance.
reference: GDPR Article 7 — withdrawal of consent; CCPA opt-out rights; CAN-SPAM Act compliance

description: Date from which this contact record became or becomes effective and valid for use in business processes. Supports temporal validity tracking for contacts associated with time-bound roles such as JV representatives, PSA counterparties, or project-specific liaisons.
reference: SAP S/4HANA BP — Valid From date; ISO 8601 date standards; PRMS temporal data management

description: Date on which this contact record expires or ceases to be valid. Null indicates an open-ended active record. Used for time-bound role assignments such as project representatives, interim contacts, or term-limited JV liaisons.
reference: SAP S/4HANA BP — Valid To date; ISO 8601 date standards; JOA counterparty representative term definitions

description: Primary business email address of the contact. Used for commercial correspondence, contract notifications, JIB distribution, AFE approvals, and regulatory communications. Serves as the primary digital identifier for this contact.
reference: GDPR Article 4; CCPA; SAP S/4HANA BP — Email Address field

description: Fax number for the contact in E.164 international format. Still used in oil and gas for formal document transmission including contract amendments, regulatory filings, and JIB (Joint Interest Billing) correspondence in certain jurisdictions.
reference: GDPR Article 4; ITU-T E.164; COPAS JIB document transmission requirements

description: Given name of the individual contact person. Used for personalized communication and formal correspondence with traders, procurement officers, operations contacts, and executive sponsors.
reference: GDPR Article 4; SAP S/4HANA BP Contact Person — First Name field

description: HSE certification or qualification held by the contact relevant to oil and gas operations (e.g., BOSIET, HUET, H2S Alive, NEBOSH, IADC WellSharp). Required for HSE liaison contacts and those involved in PTW (Permit to Work) processes or offshore operations.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.119; BSEE offshore safety training requirements; IADC WellSharp certification standards; Enablon HSE Management — Personnel Certification

business_glossary_term: Health Safety and Environment (HSE) Certification Expiry Date
description: Expiry date of the contact's primary HSE certification. Used to track certification validity and trigger renewal notifications. Contacts with expired certifications may be restricted from certain operational roles or site access.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.119; BSEE offshore safety training renewal requirements; IADC WellSharp recertification intervals

description: Indicates whether this contact is designated as an emergency response contact for HSE incidents, SIMOPS notifications, or operational emergencies. True = emergency contact available 24/7. Required for offshore and high-risk operations per BSEE and OSHA requirements.
reference: BSEE 30 CFR Part 250 — Emergency Response Plan contacts; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.119 PSM emergency contacts; Enablon HSE Management — Emergency Contact configuration

description: Indicates whether this contact is designated as the primary point of contact for the parent account. True = primary contact; False = secondary or supplementary contact. Only one contact per account should be flagged as primary at any given time.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD — Primary Contact Person designation; API counterparty management best practices

description: Official job title or position of the contact within their organization (e.g., VP Trading, Procurement Manager, HSE Director, Operations Engineer). Used for protocol, routing of communications, and authority-level determination.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD — Contact Person Title; API RP 100 Stakeholder Engagement

description: Indicates whether the contact has completed Know Your Customer (KYC) identity verification as required for counterparty onboarding and compliance. True = verified; False = pending or not verified. Supports anti-money laundering (AML) and sanctions compliance workflows.
reference: FATF Recommendations — KYC and Customer Due Diligence; OFAC sanctions screening; SEC counterparty compliance requirements; SOX Section 404

description: Date on which the contact's KYC identity verification was last completed or renewed. Used to track KYC refresh cycles and ensure compliance with periodic re-verification requirements under AML regulations.
reference: FATF Recommendations — Periodic Customer Due Diligence; OFAC compliance program requirements; FinCEN AML rules

description: ISO 639-1 two-letter language code (optionally with ISO 3166-1 country subtag) representing the contact's preferred language for communications and documents (e.g., 'en', 'ar', 'fr', 'es', 'zh-CN'). Supports multinational IOC and NOC relationship management.
reference: ISO 639-1 Language Codes; ISO 3166-1 Country Codes; SAP S/4HANA BP — Language field

description: Date of the most recent recorded business interaction with this contact (meeting, call, email exchange, contract negotiation, site visit). Used by commercial and account management teams to track relationship engagement frequency and identify dormant contacts.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD — Activity Management; CRM relationship management best practices; API counterparty engagement guidelines

description: Family name or surname of the individual contact person. Combined with first_name to form the full display name for correspondence, KYC records, and relationship management.
reference: GDPR Article 4; SAP S/4HANA BP Contact Person — Last Name field

description: LinkedIn professional profile URL for the contact. Used for relationship intelligence, executive engagement tracking, and due diligence verification of counterparty representatives in commercial and JV negotiations.
reference: GDPR Article 6 — legitimate interest basis for professional profile data; FATF KYC due diligence guidance

description: Mobile or cellular phone number for the contact in E.164 international format. Used for after-hours emergency contact, field operations coordination, and HSE incident response. Particularly important for offshore and remote operations contacts.
reference: GDPR Article 4; ITU-T E.164; BSEE 30 CFR Part 250 emergency contact requirements; OSHA emergency response

description: Free-text field for capturing relationship intelligence, communication preferences, background context, or special handling instructions for this contact. Used by commercial teams, account managers, and JV administrators to record qualitative relationship insights.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD — Contact Person Notes; GDPR Article 5 data minimization principle (notes must be relevant and not excessive)

description: The contact's preferred channel for receiving business communications. Drives automated notification routing for contract updates, invoice delivery, AFE approvals, and operational alerts.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD — Communication Method; GDPR Article 7 consent and preference management

description: Formal salutation or honorific for the contact (e.g., Mr, Ms, Dr, Eng). Used in formal correspondence, contract documents, and executive communications with senior counterparty representatives.
reference: SAP S/4HANA BP — Title/Salutation field; ISO 5218 (gender-neutral salutation guidance)

description: Indicates whether the contact has been screened against applicable sanctions lists (OFAC SDN, EU Consolidated List, UN Sanctions). True = screened and cleared; False = not yet screened or screening pending. Mandatory for counterparty onboarding in oil and gas cross-border transactions.
reference: OFAC SDN List screening requirements; EU Council Regulation (EC) No 881/2002; UN Security Council Sanctions; FATF Recommendation 6

description: Date on which the most recent sanctions screening was performed for this contact. Used to enforce periodic re-screening intervals and demonstrate compliance with OFAC and international sanctions programs.
reference: OFAC compliance program requirements; EU sanctions screening obligations; FATF Recommendation 6

description: The original identifier for this contact record in the upstream operational system of record (e.g., SAP S/4HANA Business Partner number, CRM contact ID). Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and cross-system deduplication.
reference: SAP S/4HANA BP — Business Partner Number; ISO/IEC 11179 data element identification

description: Name of the operational system of record from which this contact record originated. Supports data lineage tracking and master data management reconciliation across Oil Gas enterprise systems.
reference: ISO/IEC 11179; NIST Cybersecurity Framework — data provenance; SAP MDG (Master Data Governance) source system tracking

description: IANA time zone identifier (e.g., 'America/Houston', 'Europe/London', 'Asia/Riyadh') for the contact's primary work location. Used to schedule meetings, set communication windows, and timestamp operational notifications correctly across global operations.
reference: IANA Time Zone Database; API RP 17N operational scheduling standards

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this contact record, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change detection, data synchronization, and audit trail maintenance.
reference: SOX Section 404 — audit trail requirements; ISO 8601; GDPR Article 30 records of processing activities

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each customer or counterparty master record in the oil and gas value chain. Serves as the primary key for the SSOT of all counterparty identity data. Entity role: MASTER_PARTY.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD/FI Business Partner (BP) Master Data

description: Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Counterparties are registered within specific legal entities for tax jurisdiction, intercompany trading rules, and consolidation. Essential for JV partner accounting and transfer pricing in oil & gas

Foreign key linking to land.royalty_owner. Business justification: Royalty owners who sell their production back to the operator become commercial counterparties. Links ownership interest records to commercial sales agreements and pricing terms for equity crude marke

Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Master data linkage for entities that are both customers and vendors (common in oil & gas for JV partners, trading houses, integrated operators). Business process: unified KYC/sanctions screening, con

Foreign key linking to venture.partner. Business justification: Counterparties often hold dual roles as venture partners and commercial customers. Critical for consolidated KYC, sanctions screening, credit risk assessment, and integrated relationship management ac

SAP S/4HANA Customer Account Group code that controls the field selection, number range, and partner functions for this counterparty in the SD and FI modules. Examples include domestic customers, export customers, one-time customers, and intercompany customers. Drives master data screen layout and business process routing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Customer Account Group Configuration; SAP FI Business Partner Grouping

description: Current lifecycle status of the counterparty account. 'pending_kyc' indicates Know Your Customer (KYC) verification is in progress. 'blocked' indicates credit or compliance hold. 'suspended' indicates temporary restriction pending review. Drives order-to-cash and procure-to-pay eligibility.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Customer Master Status; OFAC Sanctions Compliance; KYC/AML Regulatory Requirements

description: Categorical classification of the counterparty within the oil and gas value chain. Distinguishes International Oil Companies (IOCs), National Oil Companies (NOCs), independent traders, national refiners, wholesale buyers, industrial consumers, retail distributors, and contract counterparties. Drives commercial terms, credit policies, and reporting segmentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: IOC|NOC|independent_trader|refiner|wholesale_buyer|industrial_consumer|retail_distributor|contract_counterparty — promote to reference product]
reference: API Industry Classification; SAP S/4HANA Customer Account Group; OPEC Member Classification

description: Timestamp when this counterparty master record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under SOX Section 404, COPAS record-keeping standards, and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: SOX Section 404 Audit Trail; SAP S/4HANA Change Document; COPAS Record Retention Standards

description: Internal credit classification of the counterparty based on external credit ratings and internal risk assessment. Drives credit limit assignment, payment terms, collateral requirements, and exposure management in oil and gas commodity trading and supply chain. Aligns with SAP Credit Management (FI-AR-CR) classifications.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Credit Management; COPAS Credit Risk Standards; Basel III Credit Risk Framework; SOX Internal Controls

description: Maximum approved credit exposure in US Dollars (USD) for this counterparty across all outstanding transactions. Enforced in SAP Credit Management (FI-AR-CR) to control order-to-cash risk in petroleum product sales, LNG offtake, and petrochemical supply agreements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Credit Management; COPAS Credit Limit Standards; Basel III Exposure at Default

description: Alphanumeric credit rating assigned by the designated credit rating agency (e.g., 'AAA', 'Baa2', 'BB+'). Used to determine counterparty creditworthiness, set credit limits, and manage financial exposure in crude oil trading, LNG offtake agreements, and downstream supply contracts.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Credit Management; Moody's/S&P/Fitch Rating Scales; Basel III Credit Risk Framework

description: Name of the external credit rating agency that issued the counterparty's credit rating (e.g., Moody's, S&P, Fitch). 'internal' indicates the rating is based solely on Oil Gas's internal credit assessment model. Used alongside credit_rating to determine credit exposure limits.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Credit Management; Basel III External Rating Requirements; COPAS Credit Procedures

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code representing the primary transaction currency agreed with this counterparty (e.g., 'USD' for US Dollar, 'EUR' for Euro). Drives foreign exchange (FX) exposure management and financial reporting under IFRS/GAAP.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA Currency Configuration; IFRS/GAAP FX Reporting; FERC Reporting

description: SAP S/4HANA customer classification code (Nielsen classification) used for statistical analysis, pricing strategy, and market segmentation of petroleum product customers. Supports downstream retail and B2B wholesale analytics.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Customer Classification; API Market Segmentation Standards

description: SAP S/4HANA Distribution Channel code defining how petroleum products are sold and delivered to this counterparty (e.g., wholesale, retail, direct, intercompany). Drives pricing condition determination and order management workflows in the SD module.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Distribution Channel Configuration; API Downstream Distribution Standards

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the country of legal domicile or principal place of business for the counterparty. Drives jurisdiction-specific regulatory compliance, sanctions screening, withholding tax rates, and export control requirements under OFAC and EAR.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 Country Codes; OFAC Sanctions List; BOEM/BSEE Jurisdiction Requirements; EPA Reporting

description: Nine-digit Data Universal Numbering System (DUNS) number assigned by Dun & Bradstreet for global business entity identification. Used for credit risk assessment, supplier qualification, and counterparty due diligence in oil and gas procurement and JV management.
reference: Dun & Bradstreet DUNS Standard; COPAS Counterparty Identification; SAP S/4HANA Business Partner DUNS

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction where the legal entity was incorporated or registered. May differ from domicile country for multinational IOCs and NOCs operating through holding structures. Used for legal entity hierarchy mapping and PSA compliance.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; IFRS/GAAP Entity Domicile; Production Sharing Agreement (PSA) Jurisdiction Rules

description: International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) code defining the default delivery and risk transfer conditions for petroleum product shipments with this counterparty. Critical for crude oil, LNG, LPG, and refined product logistics, insurance, and title transfer determination. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FOB|CIF|CFR|DAP|DDP|FCA|EXW|CPT|CIP|DAT — promote to reference product]
reference: ICC Incoterms 2020; SAP S/4HANA SD Incoterms; API Crude Oil Delivery Standards; PHMSA Pipeline Delivery Terms

description: Oil and gas value chain segment in which the counterparty primarily operates. Upstream covers Exploration and Production (E&P); midstream covers transportation and storage; downstream covers refining and marketing; petrochemicals covers chemical manufacturing. Used for commercial segmentation and pricing strategy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: upstream|midstream|downstream|petrochemicals|utilities|government|trading — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: API Industry Segmentation; SAP S/4HANA Industry Code; OPEC Value Chain Classification

description: Current status of the Know Your Customer (KYC) and Anti-Money Laundering (AML) due diligence process for this counterparty. 'verified' indicates completed and approved KYC. 'expired' indicates KYC documentation has lapsed and renewal is required. Mandatory for all counterparties under OFAC, FinCEN, and international AML regulations.
reference: OFAC Sanctions Compliance; FinCEN AML Requirements; FATF KYC Standards; SOX Section 404 Internal Controls

description: Date on which the most recent KYC/AML verification was completed and approved for this counterparty. Used to track KYC renewal cycles and ensure compliance with periodic re-verification requirements under OFAC and FinCEN regulations.
reference: OFAC Sanctions Compliance; FinCEN AML Requirements; FATF KYC Periodic Review Standards

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this counterparty master record. Used for change data capture (CDC), data lineage, and audit compliance under SOX and COPAS record-keeping requirements.
reference: SOX Section 404 Audit Trail; SAP S/4HANA Change Document; COPAS Record Retention Standards

description: Full registered legal name of the counterparty or customer as recorded in the jurisdiction of incorporation. Used for contracts, invoices, Joint Interest Billing (JIB), and regulatory filings. Corresponds to SAP BP field 'Name 1'.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Business Partner Master; COPAS Accounting Procedures; IFRS/GAAP Entity Identification

description: 20-character alphanumeric Legal Entity Identifier (LEI) issued under ISO 17442 by a Local Operating Unit (LOU) of the Global LEI System (GLEIF). Mandatory for counterparties engaged in financial instrument trading and derivatives under EMIR/Dodd-Frank. Critical for IOC and NOC counterparty identification in commodity trading.
reference: ISO 17442 Legal Entity Identifier; GLEIF Global LEI System; FERC Reporting Requirements; Dodd-Frank Act

description: Default Net Revenue Interest (NRI) percentage applicable to this counterparty for revenue distribution calculations in joint venture and production sharing arrangements. Expressed as a decimal fraction (e.g., 0.750000 = 75%). Used in Quorum Land System and SAP JVA for division order processing and revenue allocation.
reference: Quorum Land System Division Order Processing; SAP Joint Venture Accounting; COPAS NRI Calculation Standards; SEC Reserves Disclosure

description: SAP payment terms key (e.g., 'NT30', 'NT60', 'CIA') defining the agreed payment schedule for this counterparty. Governs invoice due dates, early payment discounts, and cash flow planning for crude oil, refined product, and petrochemical sales. Aligns with COPAS billing standards.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Payment Terms (ZBD1T); COPAS Accounting Procedures; IFRS/GAAP Revenue Recognition

description: Counterparty's preferred mode of petroleum product delivery. Drives logistics planning, transportation contract selection, and SCADA/DCS integration for pipeline nominations. Relevant for crude oil, refined products, LNG, LPG, and NGL delivery scheduling.
reference: PHMSA Pipeline Safety Regulations; API Petroleum Transportation Standards; SAP S/4HANA SD Shipping Conditions

description: SAP S/4HANA price group code that groups counterparties for pricing condition determination in petroleum product sales. Enables differentiated pricing strategies for IOCs, NOCs, traders, and retail distributors based on volume commitments and commercial agreements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing Conditions (VK11); API Crude Oil Pricing Standards; WTI/Brent Benchmark Pricing

description: Primary email address for commercial and operational communications with the counterparty. Used for order confirmations, invoice delivery, nomination notices, and contract correspondence in oil and gas trading and supply chain operations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Business Partner Communication; GDPR Article 4; COPAS Communication Standards

description: Primary telephone number for the counterparty's commercial or operations contact. Used for urgent operational communications including cargo nominations, delivery scheduling, and emergency HSE notifications.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Business Partner Communication; GDPR Article 4

description: SAP S/4HANA General Ledger (GL) reconciliation account code for accounts receivable postings related to this counterparty. Ensures sub-ledger to GL reconciliation for petroleum product sales revenue under IFRS/GAAP and SOX financial controls.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Reconciliation Account; IFRS/GAAP Accounts Receivable; SOX Section 404 Financial Controls; COPAS Accounting

description: City of the counterparty's official registered address. Used for jurisdictional tax determination, regulatory reporting, and legal correspondence.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Business Partner Address; ISO 3166-2 Sub-national Codes

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the counterparty's registered address. Used for sanctions screening, export control, and jurisdictional compliance.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; OFAC Sanctions Compliance; SAP S/4HANA Address Management

description: First line of the counterparty's official registered address as recorded with the relevant commercial registry. Used for legal correspondence, contract execution, and regulatory filings.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Business Partner Address; COPAS Counterparty Records; Commercial Registry Standards

description: Official company registration or incorporation number issued by the jurisdiction's commercial registry or company registrar. Used for legal entity verification, contract execution, and regulatory compliance. Distinct from tax identifiers.

description: Date on which the commercial relationship with this counterparty was formally terminated or is scheduled to end. Null for active ongoing relationships. Used for contract expiry management, credit exposure wind-down, and regulatory reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Business Partner Validity; COPAS Counterparty Offboarding; IFRS/GAAP Contract Termination

description: Date on which the commercial relationship with this counterparty was formally established. Used for relationship tenure analysis, contract anniversary tracking, and customer lifecycle management in oil and gas marketing and sales.

description: Defines the primary commercial relationship role of the counterparty with Oil Gas. A counterparty may be a customer, supplier, joint venture (JV) partner, royalty owner, government entity, or affiliate. Supports dual-role counterparties common in upstream JOA and PSA structures.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Business Partner Role; Joint Operating Agreement (JOA) Standards; Production Sharing Agreement (PSA) Frameworks

description: SAP S/4HANA Sales Organization code representing the organizational unit responsible for selling petroleum products to this counterparty. Determines pricing procedures, output determination, and revenue recognition rules applicable to this customer relationship.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Organizational Structure; IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition; API Marketing and Distribution Standards

description: Date of the most recent sanctions screening check performed against OFAC, EU, and UN sanctions lists for this counterparty. Used to enforce periodic re-screening requirements and demonstrate compliance audit trails.

description: Indicates whether this counterparty has been screened against applicable sanctions lists including OFAC SDN List, EU Consolidated Sanctions List, and UN Security Council Sanctions. reference: OFAC SDN List; EU Consolidated Sanctions List; UN Security Council Sanctions; SOX Compliance Controls

description: Government-issued Tax Identification Number (TIN), Employer Identification Number (EIN), or VAT registration number used for tax reporting, withholding obligations, and regulatory filings. Required for Joint Interest Billing (JIB) and revenue distribution under COPAS standards.

description: Operating or 'doing business as' (DBA) name used commercially, which may differ from the registered legal name. Relevant for marketing, distribution agreements, and downstream retail channel identification.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Business Partner Master; Commercial Registry Standards

description: Value Added Tax (VAT) or Goods and Services Tax (GST) registration number for the counterparty in their operating jurisdiction. Required for cross-border petroleum product invoicing, import/export documentation, and indirect tax compliance in international oil and gas operations.

description: Indicates whether this counterparty is eligible to hold a Working Interest (WI) in oil and gas joint ventures and Production Sharing Agreements (PSAs). description: Unique system-generated surrogate identifier for each credit event record. Primary key for the credit_event data product in the customer domain.

Reference to the counterparty or customer whose credit profile is affected by this event. Links to the master customer record for IOCs, NOCs, refiners, traders, wholesale buyers, or industrial consumers.

description: Reference to the specific credit account or credit facility associated with this event. A customer may have multiple credit accounts across different business lines (e.g., crude trading, LNG, refined products).
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Credit Management (FI-AR-CR); COPAS Standards

description: Foreign key linking to commercial.credit_limit. Business justification: Credit events (limit increases, decreases, breaches, rating changes) must reference the specific credit limit being modified. Required for audit trail, credit risk reporting, regulatory compliance (Ba

Foreign key linking to customer.credit_profile. Business justification: credit_event records transactional credit-related events (limit changes, rating changes, defaults) that affect a counterparty's credit profile. credit_profile is the governing parent entity. credit_ev

Reference to the original credit event that this record reverses. Populated only when reversal_flag is True. Enables bidirectional audit trail linking between original and reversal events for SOX compliance and credit history reconstruction.

The date and time when the credit event was formally approved by the designated approving authority. Distinct from event_timestamp (when the event occurred) and created_timestamp (when the record was created). Critical for SOX audit trail and segregation of duties compliance.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; SAP S/4HANA FI Credit Management; COPAS Standards

description: The name or role of the individual, committee, or system that authorized this credit event. Examples: 'Credit Risk Committee', 'VP Credit Risk', 'Automated Credit Engine', 'CFO'. Required for SOX compliance to establish accountability for credit decisions affecting financial exposure.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; COPAS Accounting Standards; SAP S/4HANA FI Credit Management

description: Indicates whether this credit event was generated automatically by the credit management system (True) or was manually initiated by a credit analyst or committee (False). Supports audit trail analysis and system performance monitoring for automated credit controls in SAP FI Credit Management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Credit Management; SOX Section 302/404; COPAS Standards

description: The internal business unit or division of Oil Gas that owns the credit relationship and is responsible for managing this credit event (e.g., 'Crude Trading', 'LNG Marketing', 'Refined Products', 'Petrochemicals', 'E&P Joint Ventures'). Used for credit risk reporting by segment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Controlling; IFRS 8 Operating Segments; COPAS Standards

description: The monetary value of collateral demanded, posted, or released in connection with this credit event. Applicable for COLLATERAL_CALL and COLLATERAL_RELEASE event types. Includes letters of credit, bank guarantees, cash deposits, or parent company guarantees used to secure credit exposure in oil and gas trading.

description: Classification of the collateral instrument associated with this credit event. LETTER_OF_CREDIT: documentary credit from a bank; BANK_GUARANTEE: unconditional bank guarantee; CASH_DEPOSIT: cash held as security; PARENT_GUARANTEE: guarantee from parent company of counterparty; PREPAYMENT: advance payment securing future deliveries. Null for non-collateral events.

description: The reference number of the underlying commercial contract (e.g., crude oil sale agreement, LNG SPA, refined products supply contract, PSA, JOA) associated with this credit event. Provides traceability from the credit event back to the originating commercial agreement.

description: The date and time when this credit event record was first created in the system. Serves as the record audit creation timestamp for data lineage, SOX compliance, and lakehouse silver layer audit trail. Distinct from event_timestamp (when the business event occurred).

description: The numeric credit limit in the designated currency that was in place before this event. Populated specifically for CREDIT_LIMIT_CHANGE events to enable quantitative analysis of limit movements. Null for non-limit events.

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to monetary amounts in this credit event (e.g., USD for US Dollar, EUR for Euro, GBP for British Pound). Supports multi-currency credit management for international counterparties in crude oil, LNG, and refined products trading.

description: The number of calendar days the overdue payment has exceeded its contractual due date at the time of this credit event. Used for aging analysis, IFRS 9 expected credit loss staging (Stage 1/2/3), and credit risk reporting. Null for non-payment events.

description: The business date on which the credit event takes effect and the new credit terms or conditions become operative. May differ from event_timestamp when events are backdated or scheduled for future activation (e.g., a credit limit change approved today but effective at month-end).

description: Externally-visible, human-readable business identifier for the credit event, formatted as CE-YYYY-NNNNNN. Used in correspondence, audit trails, and SOX compliance documentation to uniquely reference a specific credit action.

description: Current lifecycle state of the credit event record. PENDING: event initiated but awaiting approval; ACTIVE: event is in effect; COMPLETED: event has been fully processed and closed; REVERSED: event was reversed due to error or dispute; CANCELLED: event was cancelled before taking effect.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Credit Management; SOX Section 302/404; COPAS Standards

description: The precise date and time when the credit event occurred in the real world (e.g., when the credit hold was placed, when the rating downgrade was confirmed, when the payment default was declared). This is the principal business event time, distinct from record audit timestamps.

description: Classification of the credit-related event that occurred. Drives downstream workflow and reporting. CREDIT_LIMIT_CHANGE: adjustment to approved credit limit; RATING_CHANGE: upgrade or downgrade of internal/external credit rating; COLLATERAL_CALL: demand for additional collateral; CREDIT_HOLD: suspension of credit facility; CREDIT_RELEASE: reinstatement of credit facility; PAYMENT_DEFAULT: counterparty failure to pay within contractual terms. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CREDIT_LIMIT_CHANGE|RATING_CHANGE|COLLATERAL_CALL|CREDIT_HOLD|CREDIT_RELEASE|PAYMENT_DEFAULT|CREDIT_REVIEW_COMPLETED|CREDIT_WATCH|COVENANT_BREACH|MARGIN_CALL — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Credit Management; IFRS 9 Financial Instruments; COPAS Standards

description: The date on which the credit event condition expires or is scheduled for review. Applicable for temporary credit holds, collateral calls with deadlines, or time-limited credit limit changes. Null for permanent events.

description: The total outstanding credit exposure to the counterparty at the time of this credit event, expressed in the event currency. reference: IFRS 9 Financial Instruments; SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Credit Management; COPAS Standards; SOX Section 302/404

description: Indicates whether the counterparty's Know Your Customer (KYC) verification was current and valid at the time of this credit event. A value of True confirms KYC compliance; False indicates KYC was expired or incomplete, which may have contributed to a credit hold or review.

description: The updated value of the credit attribute after this event is applied. Stores the after-state for audit trail purposes. For CREDIT_LIMIT_CHANGE, this is the new limit amount as a string; for RATING_CHANGE, this is the new rating code (e.g., 'BB-'); for CREDIT_HOLD, this is the new status. Stored as STRING to accommodate heterogeneous event types.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; COPAS Accounting Standards; SAP S/4HANA FI Credit Management

description: Free-text field for additional commentary, context, or instructions related to this credit event. May include analyst observations, counterparty communications summary, escalation notes, or remediation actions. Supports credit review documentation and audit trail completeness.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; COPAS Accounting Standards; SAP S/4HANA FI Credit Management

description: The monetary value of the overdue or defaulted payment that triggered a PAYMENT_DEFAULT credit event. Represents the outstanding invoice amount not paid within contractual terms. Null for non-default events.
reference: IFRS 9 Financial Instruments; SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; COPAS JIB Standards; SOX Section 302/404

description: The prior value of the credit attribute before this event was applied. Stores the before-state for audit trail purposes. For CREDIT_LIMIT_CHANGE, this is the prior limit amount as a string; for RATING_CHANGE, this is the prior rating code (e.g., 'BBB+'); for CREDIT_HOLD, this is the prior status. Stored as STRING to accommodate heterogeneous event types.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; COPAS Accounting Standards; SAP S/4HANA FI Credit Management

description: The source of the credit rating referenced in this event. INTERNAL: proprietary internal credit scoring model; SP: S&P Global Ratings; MOODYS: Moody's Investors Service; FITCH: Fitch Ratings; DBRS: DBRS Morningstar. Applicable for RATING_CHANGE events. Null for non-rating events.

description: The credit rating code assigned to the counterparty after this rating change event (e.g., 'BB+', 'Ba1', 'BBB-'). Follows the rating scale of the designated rating_agency. Null for non-rating events.

description: The credit rating code assigned to the counterparty before this rating change event (e.g., 'BBB+', 'Baa1', 'A-'). Follows the rating scale of the designated rating_agency. Null for non-rating events.

description: Indicates whether this credit event has been reversed (True) due to error, dispute resolution, or system correction. When True, the reversed_event_id field identifies the original event being reversed. Supports SOX audit trail integrity.

description: The scheduled date by which the next credit review must be completed following this event. description: The document number in the originating source system (e.g., SAP FI document number, trading system transaction ID) that corresponds to this credit event. Enables cross-system traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and operational systems of record.

description: The originating system that generated or recorded this credit event. description: Structured classification of the root cause category that triggered this credit event. Enables trend analysis and portfolio-level credit risk reporting. Date on which the credit limit and terms were formally approved by the credit analyst or credit committee. Establishes the effective start of the current credit terms for this counterparty.

'letter_of_credit' = bank-issued LC; 'parent_guarantee' = corporate guarantee from parent entity; 'prepayment' = advance payment required; 'cash_deposit' = cash margin held; 'surety_bond' = third-party bond; 'none' = unsecured. Common in crude oil trading and LNG offtake agreements.

description: Indicates whether the current credit limit and terms have been formally approved by the credit committee, as required for counterparties above the credit analyst's delegated authority threshold. True = committee-approved; False = within analyst authority or pending approval.

description: Current total outstanding credit exposure in US Dollars, representing the sum of open invoices, unconfirmed deliveries, and mark-to-market positions for crude oil, LNG, and refined product transactions with this counterparty.

description: Reason code for the active credit hold. 'overdue_payment' = outstanding invoices past due; 'limit_exceeded' = exposure exceeds approved limit; 'rating_downgrade' = credit rating fell below threshold; 'kyc_failure' = KYC compliance issue; 'sanctions_flag' = OFAC/sanctions screening hit; 'manual_hold' = credit analyst discretionary hold. Null when credit_hold_flag is False.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Credit Management; OFAC Sanctions Compliance; FATF KYC Guidelines

description: Maximum approved credit exposure in US Dollars for this counterparty across all crude oil trading, LNG offtake, and refined product wholesale transactions. Set by the credit committee and reviewed periodically. Denominated in USD as the functional currency for international petroleum transactions.

description: Free-text field for credit analyst commentary, special conditions, exceptions, or contextual information relevant to the credit profile. May include notes on collateral negotiations, payment dispute history, or market intelligence affecting creditworthiness.

description: Scheduled frequency at which this counterparty's credit profile is formally reviewed and reaffirmed by the credit risk team. 'event_triggered' indicates review is initiated by a specific event (e.g., rating downgrade, payment default, material contract change) rather than a fixed schedule.

description: Ratio of current credit exposure to approved credit limit, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 75.5000 = 75.5%). Used to monitor proximity to credit limit thresholds and trigger review or hold actions in crude oil and refined product trading operations.

description: Indicates whether the counterparty is on credit watch and the directional outlook. 'none' = no watch; 'positive' = potential upgrade; 'negative' = potential downgrade; 'developing' = uncertain direction. Mirrors external agency watch terminology and is applied to both internal and external ratings.

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the credit limit and exposure amounts (typically USD for international petroleum transactions, but may be EUR, GBP, or local currency for specific regional contracts).

description: Days Sales Outstanding (DSO) — the average number of days this counterparty takes to pay invoices, calculated over the trailing review period. A key credit risk indicator for crude oil, LNG, and refined product wholesale transactions. Higher DSO indicates slower payment behavior.
reference: COPAS Petroleum Accounting Standards; IFRS 9 Financial Instruments; SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Analytics

description: Date from which the current credit terms (limit, rating, collateral) are binding and applicable to transactions with this counterparty. Supports temporal validity tracking for credit agreements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Credit Management; IFRS 9 Financial Instruments; COPAS Petroleum Accounting Standards

description: Date until which the current credit terms are valid. Null for open-ended credit arrangements. When populated, triggers renewal workflow before expiry to avoid lapse in credit coverage for active crude oil and LNG trading counterparties.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Credit Management; IFRS 9 Financial Instruments; COPAS Petroleum Accounting Standards

description: Date on which the most recent external credit rating (S&P, Moody's, or Fitch) was issued or last confirmed. Used to assess rating currency and trigger re-evaluation when ratings are stale relative to credit review policy.
reference: IFRS 9 Financial Instruments; SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Credit Management

description: External credit rating assigned by Fitch Ratings for the counterparty (e.g., AAA, AA+, BBB-, BB, CCC). Provides a third-agency perspective for credit risk triangulation on significant crude oil trading and LNG offtake counterparties.

description: Proprietary internal credit rating assigned by the company's credit risk team, reflecting the counterparty's creditworthiness based on financial analysis, payment history, and industry position. Uses a letter-grade scale (e.g., AAA, AA+, BB-) aligned with internal credit policy.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Credit Management; COPAS Petroleum Accounting Standards; SOX Section 404; IFRS 9

description: Date on which the internal credit rating was last assigned or reaffirmed by the credit risk team. Drives the credit review cycle and next review date calculation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Credit Management; COPAS Petroleum Accounting Standards; SOX Section 404

description: Date on which the current KYC verification expires and renewal is required. Drives compliance workflow alerts to ensure continuous KYC coverage for active crude oil, LNG, and refined product trading counterparties.

description: Know Your Customer (KYC) compliance status for this counterparty. 'verified' = KYC checks passed and current; 'pending' = KYC in progress; 'expired' = KYC verification has lapsed and renewal is required; 'failed' = KYC checks failed; 'not_required' = exempt per policy. Mandatory for all trading counterparties under FATF and OFAC compliance frameworks.
reference: FATF Recommendations; OFAC Sanctions Compliance; FinCEN AML Guidelines; SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Credit Management

description: Date on which the most recent formal credit review was completed and the credit profile was last reaffirmed or updated by the credit analyst or credit committee.

description: External credit rating assigned by Moody's Investors Service for the counterparty (e.g., Aaa, Aa1, Baa2, Ba3, Caa1). Used alongside S&P and Fitch ratings for comprehensive external credit assessment of major trading counterparties.

description: Scheduled date for the next formal credit review of this counterparty. Calculated from last_review_date and credit_review_frequency. Used to drive workflow alerts in SAP S/4HANA and credit management dashboards.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Credit Management; COPAS Petroleum Accounting Standards; SOX Section 404

description: Quantitative score (typically 0–100) reflecting the counterparty's historical payment behavior, incorporating on-time payment rate, frequency of late payments, and dispute history across crude oil, LNG, and refined product transactions. description: SAP payment terms code defining the credit period and discount structure for this counterparty (e.g., NT30 = net 30 days, NT60 = net 60 days). Governs invoice due date calculation for crude oil liftings, LNG cargo invoices, and refined product deliveries.

description: Externally-known business identifier for the credit profile, used in credit review correspondence, JIB (Joint Interest Billing) documentation, and counterparty communications. Format: CP- followed by 6–12 alphanumeric characters.

description: Current lifecycle status of the credit profile record. 'active' indicates the profile is in force; 'suspended' indicates a temporary hold; 'under_review' indicates a scheduled or triggered reassessment; 'closed' indicates the counterparty relationship has ended; 'pending_approval' indicates awaiting credit committee sign-off.

description: Internal risk tier classification for the counterparty, used to apply differentiated credit policies, collateral requirements, and monitoring intensity. description: Date on which the most recent sanctions screening was performed against OFAC SDN, EU, and UN sanctions lists. Used to ensure screening currency and compliance with regulatory requirements for international petroleum trading.

description: Result of the most recent OFAC and international sanctions screening for this counterparty. 'clear' = no matches; 'flagged' = potential match requiring review; 'under_review' = compliance team investigating; 'blocked' = confirmed sanctions match, all transactions prohibited.

description: External credit rating assigned by S&P Global Ratings for the counterparty (e.g., AAA, AA+, BBB-, BB, CCC). Used to supplement internal ratings for large IOC, NOC, and refiner counterparties in crude oil and LNG offtake agreements. Null if no S&P rating exists.

Indicates whether adverse media screening has identified negative news, regulatory actions, legal proceedings, or reputational risk associated with the counterparty or its beneficial owners. Adverse media findings may trigger enhanced due diligence or relationship termination.
