description: Version of the FATF Recommendations framework under which this KYC record was prepared and assessed (e.g., FATF 2012 Revised Recommendations, FATF 2023 Update). Ensures traceability of compliance assessments to the applicable regulatory standard version.
reference: FATF Recommendations (2012, as revised); Internal Compliance Framework Versioning Policy

description: Risk classification of the counterparty's country of incorporation based on FATF grey/black lists, OFAC sanctioned country lists, and internal country risk policy. Drives KYC tier assignment and enhanced due diligence requirements.
reference: FATF High-Risk Jurisdictions; OFAC Sanctioned Countries List; EU High-Risk Third Countries List

description: Externally-known alphanumeric reference number assigned to this KYC compliance record, used for audit trails, regulatory submissions, and compliance officer tracking. Format: KYC- followed by alphanumeric characters.
reference: FATF Recommendation 10; OFAC Compliance Framework; FinCEN Customer Due Diligence Rule

description: Current lifecycle status of the KYC compliance record indicating whether the counterparty has passed, is under review, or has failed the KYC process. Drives trading eligibility and credit decisions.
reference: FATF Recommendation 10; OFAC Compliance Framework; FinCEN Customer Due Diligence Rule

business_glossary_term: Know Your Customer (KYC) Due Diligence Tier
description: Classification of the level of due diligence applied to this counterparty. Enhanced Due Diligence (EDD) applies to high-risk counterparties such as state-owned NOCs, PEPs, or entities in high-risk jurisdictions. Standard applies to typical IOC/trading counterparties. Simplified applies to low-risk, regulated entities.
reference: FATF Recommendation 10; FATF Recommendation 19; EU 4th Anti-Money Laundering Directive (AMLD4); FinCEN Customer Due Diligence Rule

description: Date on which the most recent periodic KYC review was completed. Distinct from verification_date (initial verification) and next_review_date (scheduled future review). Used to track review cadence compliance and identify overdue reviews.
reference: FATF Recommendation 10; Internal KYC Periodic Review Policy

description: Scheduled date for the next periodic KYC review. May differ from expiry_date if an interim review is triggered by a risk event, sanctions alert, or change in counterparty ownership structure.
reference: FATF Recommendation 10; Internal KYC Periodic Review Policy; OFAC Compliance Framework

business_glossary_term: Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) Sanctions Screening Result
description: Result of screening the counterparty against the U.S. Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) and Blocked Persons List. A confirmed match prohibits all transactions. Potential matches require compliance officer review.
reference: OFAC SDN List; 31 CFR Parts 500-598; Executive Orders on Sanctions; Internal Sanctions Compliance Policy

description: Date on which the counterparty was first onboarded into the KYC compliance program. Represents the start of the commercial relationship from a compliance perspective and anchors the KYC lifecycle timeline.
reference: FATF Recommendation 10; FinCEN Customer Due Diligence Rule; Internal Counterparty Onboarding Policy

description: Specific category of PEP classification when pep_flag is true. Distinguishes between domestic PEPs (e.g., government ministers), foreign PEPs (e.g., heads of state), international organization PEPs, close associates, and family members. Relevant for NOC counterparties with government-affiliated executives.
reference: FATF Recommendation 12; EU AMLD4 Article 3(9); FinCEN Customer Due Diligence Rule

description: Indicates whether the counterparty or any of its beneficial owners is classified as a Politically Exposed Person (PEP) — a current or former senior government official, state-owned enterprise executive, or close associate. PEP status mandates Enhanced Due Diligence (EDD) per FATF Recommendation 12.
reference: FATF Recommendation 12; EU AMLD4 Article 20; FinCEN Customer Due Diligence Rule

description: Free-text description of the reason for KYC rejection when kyc_status is 'rejected'. Documents the specific compliance deficiency, sanctions match, or documentation failure that resulted in rejection. Required for audit trail and regulatory reporting.
reference: FATF Recommendation 10; Internal KYC Rejection Documentation Policy; SOX Section 302

description: Reason that initiated the most recent KYC review. Distinguishes between scheduled periodic reviews and event-driven reviews triggered by sanctions alerts, changes in beneficial ownership, adverse media findings, risk escalations, or regulatory requests.
reference: FATF Recommendation 10; Internal KYC Event-Driven Review Policy; OFAC Compliance Framework

description: Numeric composite risk score (0.00–100.00) calculated from the weighted combination of jurisdiction risk, AML risk rating, PEP status, sanctions exposure, adverse media findings, and beneficial ownership complexity. Higher scores indicate greater compliance risk. Used to prioritize enhanced due diligence and monitoring resources.
reference: FATF Risk-Based Approach Guidance; Internal KYC Risk Scoring Methodology; Wolfsberg Group AML Principles

description: Most recent date on which the counterparty was screened against OFAC, EU, and UN sanctions lists. Sanctions screening must be refreshed at defined intervals and upon any material change in counterparty ownership or jurisdiction.
reference: OFAC Compliance Framework; EU Consolidated Sanctions List; UN Security Council Sanctions; Internal Sanctions Screening Policy

description: Reference identifier of this KYC record in the originating compliance management system (e.g., Enablon HSE Management or dedicated KYC platform). Enables traceability from the Silver layer back to the operational system of record for audit and reconciliation purposes.
reference: Internal Data Lineage and Audit Policy; SOX Section 404; NIST Cybersecurity Framework

description: Indicates whether the counterparty is currently eligible for crude oil, natural gas, LNG, NGL, or refined product trading based on the current KYC status, sanctions screening results, AML risk rating, and document collection completeness. Set to false when KYC is expired, rejected, or sanctions match is confirmed.
reference: OFAC Compliance Framework; FATF Recommendation 10; Internal Trading Counterparty Eligibility Policy

description: Number of ultimate beneficial owners (UBOs) identified and disclosed for this counterparty. A UBO is any natural person holding 25% or more direct or indirect ownership or control. Used to assess ownership complexity and completeness of beneficial ownership disclosure.
reference: FATF Recommendation 24; FinCEN Customer Due Diligence Rule 31 CFR 1010.230; EU AMLD4 Article 30

description: Result of screening the counterparty against the United Nations Security Council consolidated sanctions list. Applies globally to all counterparties regardless of jurisdiction. A confirmed match prohibits all transactions under international law.
reference: UN Security Council Consolidated Sanctions List; UN Charter Article 41; Internal Sanctions Compliance Policy

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this KYC record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Updated on every status change, review completion, sanctions screening refresh, or document update. Supports change tracking and audit compliance.
reference: SOX Section 404; NIST Cybersecurity Framework; Internal Data Governance Audit Policy

description: Date on which the KYC verification process was completed and the counterparty's identity and documentation were confirmed by the compliance team. Marks the start of the KYC validity period.
reference: FATF Recommendation 10; FinCEN Customer Due Diligence Rule; OFAC Compliance Framework

description: Date on which the compliance waiver expires when waiver_flag is true. After this date, trading eligibility is automatically suspended until full KYC compliance is restored. Null when no waiver is in effect.
reference: Internal KYC Waiver Policy; FATF Risk-Based Approach Guidance

description: Indicates whether a formal compliance waiver has been granted for this KYC record, allowing temporary trading eligibility despite incomplete documentation or pending review. Waivers require senior compliance authority approval and are time-limited.
reference: Internal KYC Waiver Policy; FATF Risk-Based Approach Guidance; SOX Section 302

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each lifting schedule record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for the lifting_schedule data product. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete planned/actual cargo lifting event with a full lifecycle.
reference: COPAS Petroleum Accounting Standards; SAP S/4HANA SD Module

description: Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: customer_lifting_schedule tracks planned and actual liftings for a customer account against a term offtake agreement. Despite being explicitly described as a customer-facing schedule, it has no direct

Foreign key linking to exploration.block. Business justification: Lifting schedules are organized by producing block for production allocation, overlift/underlift tracking, and government reporting. Block-level lifting data is required for JOA cash calls and PSA cos

Foreign key linking to product.certificate_of_quality. Business justification: Each actual cargo lifting requires a certificate of quality issued at loading for custody transfer. Essential for contractual compliance verification, customs clearance documentation, payment settleme

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Lifting operations (terminal handling, vessel coordination, demurrage) incur direct costs allocated to operational cost centers for crude marketing cost control and margin analysis.

Foreign key linking to venture.joa. Business justification: Equity crude liftings are governed by JOA terms including lifting procedures, nomination deadlines, and entitlement calculations. Essential for JOA-compliant lifting program execution, partner entitle

Foreign key linking to land.lease. Business justification: Lifting schedules execute lease-specific production entitlements for equity crude offtake. Core to revenue realization, entitlement balancing, and ensuring each partner lifts their entitled share per

Foreign key linking to venture.partner. Business justification: Lifting schedules track actual cargo liftings by entitled venture partners. Business requires partner-level lifting records for overlift/underlift reconciliation, imbalance settlement, and JIB reporti

Foreign key linking to customer.delivery_point. Business justification: Loading port should be a FK to delivery_point (which represents terminals/ports). This replaces the loading_port_name and loading_country_code strings with a proper relational link. Note: labeled as l

Foreign key linking to customer.nomination. Business justification: A lifting schedule is typically created FROM a customer nomination. This parent-child relationship links the planned schedule to the originating nomination request. The FK may be NULL initially if the

Foreign key linking to customer.offtake_entitlement. Business justification: A customer lifting schedule is executed against a specific offtake entitlement — the entitlement defines the volume, product, and terms that the lifting schedule fulfills. The description explicitly s
Reference to the hydrocarbon product being lifted (e.g., crude oil grade, LNG, NGL, condensate). Links to the product master for grade specifications, quality parameters, and pricing basis.
reference: API MPMS; SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master; Avocet Production Operations

description: Foreign key linking to reservoir.production_forecast. Business justification: Customer lifting schedules are operationally derived from reservoir production forecasts — schedulers allocate cargo liftings against forecast production volumes. This link supports the monthly liftin

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Crude liftings generate revenue recognized at field/asset level profit centers for upstream P&L reporting, production entitlement accounting, and field-level economics (netback analysis).

Foreign key linking to venture.psa. Business justification: PSA contracts govern cost recovery oil and profit oil lifting entitlements and schedules. Required for PSA entitlement calculation, contractor share determination, government take allocation, and fisc

Foreign key linking to procurement.contract. Business justification: Lifting schedules execute under supply or processing contracts that govern crude grades, delivery windows, and pricing mechanisms. Required for contract performance tracking, demurrage calculations, a
Reference to the terminal or loading port facility from which the cargo is lifted. Determines applicable measurement procedures, custody transfer protocols, and port authority requirements.
reference: API MPMS Chapter 17 — Marine Measurement; BSEE offshore terminal regulations; SAP S/4HANA Plant/Storage Location

description: Reference to the nominated vessel (tanker, LNG carrier, or FPSO shuttle tanker) assigned to perform the lifting. Links to vessel master for IMO number, capacity, and flag state details.
reference: IMO vessel identification standards; PHMSA maritime regulations; SAP S/4HANA Transportation Management

description: Actual volume of hydrocarbon product lifted at the terminal as measured by the custody transfer metering system, expressed in the unit defined by volume_unit. Populated upon completion of loading and issuance of the bill of lading. Null until lifting is completed.
reference: API MPMS Chapter 17 — Marine Measurement; ISO 91; Avocet Production Operations — actual lifted volume

description: Actual calendar date on which the cargo lifting commenced at the terminal or loading port. Compared against scheduled_lifting_date to compute lifting variance and assess operational performance. Null until lifting is in-progress or completed.
reference: Avocet Production Operations — actual lifting date; API MPMS Chapter 17

description: API gravity of the crude oil or condensate cargo as measured at the loading terminal, expressed in degrees API. Determines crude quality classification (light, medium, heavy), pricing differential, and refinery yield optimization. Not applicable for LNG/gas liftings.
reference: API MPMS Chapter 9 — Density Determination; API gravity scale (API RP 11B); Platts crude quality assessments

description: Date on which the bill of lading was issued, representing the official date of title transfer and the revenue recognition date under IFRS 15 / US GAAP for FOB cargo sales. Null until lifting is completed and B/L is issued.
reference: IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition; SEC reserves disclosure; SAP S/4HANA SD Billing

description: Official bill of lading number issued by the vessel master or terminal operator upon completion of cargo loading. Serves as the primary legal document for title transfer, revenue recognition, and customs/export documentation. Critical for IFRS 15 revenue recognition trigger.
reference: IFRS 15 — Revenue Recognition at point of title transfer; API MPMS Chapter 17; UCP 600 documentary credit rules

description: Basic sediment and water content of the crude oil cargo expressed as a weight or volume percentage, as determined by the custody transfer measurement at the loading terminal. Deducted from gross standard volume to arrive at net standard volume for invoicing.
reference: API MPMS Chapter 10 — Sediment and Water Determination; ASTM D4007; API RP 45

description: Unique cargo reference number assigned by the seller or trading desk for internal tracking of this specific cargo parcel. Used for cross-referencing with trading systems, back-office settlement, and cargo documentation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Sales Order; Avocet Production Operations — cargo reference; COPAS cargo tracking

description: Timestamp when this lifting schedule record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance reporting. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category.
reference: SOX Section 404; IFRS 15; SAP S/4HANA audit log

description: Free-text description of the reason for deferring a scheduled lifting (e.g., vessel unavailability, force majeure, terminal maintenance, buyer request, weather conditions). Populated only when lifting_status is 'deferred'. Used for operational analysis and contractual force majeure documentation.
reference: Avocet Production Operations — deferral tracking; standard SPA force majeure provisions; BSEE incident reporting

description: Indicates whether demurrage charges are applicable for this lifting due to vessel detention beyond the agreed laytime at the loading terminal. True when actual time on berth exceeds the allowed laytime per the charter party or sale agreement.
reference: Standard crude oil SPA demurrage provisions; Incoterms 2020; PHMSA maritime cargo regulations

description: Number of hours of demurrage incurred by the vessel at the loading terminal beyond the agreed laytime, as calculated from the Notice of Readiness (NOR) and Statement of Facts. Used to calculate demurrage charges payable under the charter party.
reference: Standard crude oil SPA laytime and demurrage clauses; Incoterms 2020; BIMCO laytime definitions

description: Contractual volume entitlement for this lifting as specified in the term offtake agreement or spot sale nomination, expressed in the unit defined by volume_unit (BBL or MT). Represents the buyer's right to lift under the agreement for this lifting window. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER QUANTITATIVE_RESULT category.
reference: COPAS PASC; SAP S/4HANA SD Quantity Contract; Avocet Production Operations — entitlement volume

description: Total volume of the cargo as measured at observed temperature and pressure conditions at the loading terminal, before correction to standard conditions. Expressed in the unit defined by volume_unit. Used in custody transfer calculations per API MPMS.
reference: API MPMS Chapter 11 — Physical Properties; API MPMS Chapter 12 — Calculation of Petroleum Quantities; ISO 91

description: Volume of the cargo corrected to standard conditions (60°F / 15°C at 1 atm) using volume correction factors per API MPMS Chapter 11. The primary billing and revenue recognition volume for crude oil custody transfers.
reference: API MPMS Chapter 11 — Volume Correction Factors (VCF); API MPMS Chapter 12; ISO 91 petroleum measurement

description: International Commercial Terms (Incoterms 2020) code defining the point of title transfer, risk transfer, and cost allocation between seller and buyer for this cargo lifting. FOB (Free on Board) is most common for crude oil liftings; CIF/CFR for delivered cargoes.
reference: Incoterms 2020 (ICC); IFRS 15 — performance obligation and title transfer; SAP S/4HANA SD Incoterms

description: End date of the laycan window — the latest date by which the nominated vessel must arrive at the loading port. If the vessel arrives after this date, the seller may have the right to cancel the cargo. Critical for demurrage and laytime calculations.
reference: Incoterms 2020; standard crude oil SPA terms; PHMSA maritime cargo regulations

description: Start date of the laycan (laydays cancelling) window — the earliest date the nominated vessel may arrive at the loading port to commence lifting operations. Defines the contractual arrival window for the vessel under the cargo sale terms.
reference: Incoterms 2020 FOB/CIF terms; API MPMS Chapter 17; standard crude oil sale and purchase agreement (SPA) terms

description: Externally-known business identifier for the lifting event, typically assigned by the terminal operator or production operator. Used for cross-referencing with counterparty documents, bill of lading, and cargo tracking systems. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.
reference: API RP 19C; Avocet Production Operations — cargo lifting reference; SAP S/4HANA SD Delivery Document

description: Sequential number of this lifting within the contract year or agreement period (e.g., 1st lifting, 2nd lifting). Used for tracking cumulative entitlement consumption, scheduling periodic liftings under annual offtake agreements, and partner allocation reporting.
reference: Avocet Production Operations — lifting sequence; COPAS annual lifting schedule

description: Current workflow state of the lifting event in its operational lifecycle. Drives production allocation, revenue recognition triggers, and inventory management. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER LIFECYCLE_STATUS category.
reference: Avocet Production Operations — lifting status workflow; IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition

description: Contractual tolerance percentage (e.g., ±5%) applied to the entitlement volume within which the actual lifted volume is considered compliant with the offtake agreement. Volumes outside this tolerance may trigger over/under-lift balancing obligations.
reference: COPAS over/under-lift accounting; JOA lifting tolerance provisions; SAP Joint Venture Accounting

description: Gross standard volume minus the volume of sediment, water, and free water (BS&W deductions) at standard conditions. Represents the net merchantable hydrocarbon volume for invoicing, revenue recognition, and production allocation reporting.
reference: API MPMS Chapter 10 — Sediment and Water; API MPMS Chapter 12; COPAS production allocation standards

description: Contractual deadline by which the buyer must nominate the lifting vessel to the seller or terminal operator. Failure to nominate by this date may result in forfeiture of the lifting entitlement or deferral under the offtake agreement terms.
reference: Standard crude oil SPA nomination provisions; Avocet Production Operations — nomination management

description: Classification of the lifting outcome relative to entitlement: over_lift (actual > entitlement + tolerance), under_lift (actual < entitlement - tolerance), balanced (within tolerance), or pending (lifting not yet completed). Drives JOA partner balancing and make-up lifting obligations.
reference: COPAS over/under-lift accounting standards; SAP Joint Venture Accounting — partner balancing; JOA lifting provisions

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the cargo price and invoice are denominated (e.g., USD for US Dollar, EUR for Euro). Most international crude oil and LNG trades are denominated in USD.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS 21 Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates; SAP S/4HANA FI currency configuration

description: Contractual premium or discount applied to the reference pricing basis for this cargo, expressed in USD per BBL or USD per MT. Reflects crude quality differentials, location adjustments, and commercial negotiation outcomes. Confidential commercial term.
reference: IFRS 15 variable consideration; SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing Conditions; Platts differential assessments

description: Reference price benchmark against which the cargo price is calculated (e.g., Dated Brent, WTI, Dubai/Oman, Platts Oman, JKM for LNG). Determines the pricing formula applied for revenue recognition and invoice generation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: dated_brent|wti|dubai_oman|platts_oman|argus_sour|lng_jkm|lng_ttf — promote to reference product]
reference: Platts crude oil price assessments; Argus Media; ICE Brent benchmark; CME WTI benchmark; IFRS 15 variable consideration

description: Planned calendar date on which the cargo lifting is scheduled to commence at the terminal or loading port, as agreed in the term offtake agreement or spot sale nomination. Used for production planning and inventory management. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category (day-level planning precision).
reference: Avocet Production Operations — lifting schedule; API MPMS Chapter 17

description: Sulfur content of the crude oil cargo expressed as a weight percentage (wt%). Classifies crude as sweet (<0.5% sulfur) or sour (>0.5% sulfur), directly impacting pricing differential, refinery processing requirements, and environmental compliance under IMO 2020 sulfur regulations.
reference: API MPMS Chapter 10 — Sediment and Water; IMO MARPOL Annex VI sulfur regulations; EPA Clean Air Act sulfur standards; ASTM D4294

description: Timestamp when this lifting schedule record was last modified. Tracks schedule revisions, status changes, and volume adjustments for audit and data lineage purposes. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category.
reference: SOX Section 404; SAP S/4HANA change log

description: International Maritime Organization (IMO) unique vessel identification number in the format IMO followed by 7 digits. Provides a globally unique, permanent vessel identifier for regulatory reporting, sanctions screening, and cargo tracking.
reference: IMO Resolution A.1078(28) — ship identification number scheme; PHMSA maritime regulations; OFAC sanctions screening

description: Unit of measure for all volume fields on this lifting record. BBL (barrels) is standard for crude oil and condensate; MT (metric tonnes) is standard for LNG and some crude trades; MMBTU for gas energy content; MCF for gas volume. Ensures consistent interpretation of entitlement and actual volume fields.
reference: API MPMS Chapter 11 — Volume Correction; ISO 91 petroleum measurement standards

description: Difference between actual_lifted_volume and entitlement_volume, expressed in the unit defined by volume_unit. Positive value indicates over-lift; negative value indicates under-lift. Used for production allocation reconciliation, over/under-lift accounting, and partner balancing under JOA terms.
reference: COPAS over/under-lift accounting; SAP Joint Venture Accounting — partner balancing; JOA lifting tolerance provisions

description: Primary key for customer_term_contract
Foreign key linking to the commercial account that is party to this term contract

Foreign key linking to exploration.block. Business justification: Term contracts for crude sales reference the producing block for production origin certification, government export reporting, and revenue allocation. Block-level contract tracking is required for JOA

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Term contracts allocate fulfillment costs (logistics, quality assurance, contract administration) to cost centers for contract profitability analysis and cost-to-serve tracking in commercial operation

Foreign key linking to product.crude_grade. Business justification: Crude oil term contracts specify the exact crude grade (Brent, WTI, Dubai) being sold. Essential for pricing differentials, API gravity bands, sulfur content specifications, and delivery scheduling. E

Foreign key linking to customer.delivery_point. Business justification: A customer term contract specifies delivery terms and locations for the contracted volumes. Adding delivery_point_id links the term contract to its designated delivery location, enabling operational s

Foreign key linking to reservoir.development_plan. Business justification: Long-term customer supply contracts are negotiated and committed based on field development plans. Commercial teams reference the development plan's production profile, plateau rates, and EUR when str

Foreign key linking to exploration.discovery. Business justification: When a discovery is declared commercial, term sales contracts are negotiated for future production offtake. Commercial teams reference the specific discovery in term contracts for revenue attribution,

Foreign key linking to exploration.license. Business justification: Term crude sales contracts are scoped to production from a specific license area. The license defines fiscal regime, royalty obligations, and export rights that govern the contract terms. Revenue attr

Foreign key linking to land.operator. Business justification: Term contracts often specify the operating entity responsible for production and delivery of contracted volumes. Essential for contract administration, operational coordination, and ensuring the corre
Foreign key linking to the petroleum product covered by this term contract

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Term contracts contribute revenue and margin to profit centers for segment reporting, contract portfolio performance tracking, and business unit P&L. Critical for commercial performance management.

Foreign key linking to product.quality_spec. Business justification: Term contracts reference formal quality specifications for contractual acceptance criteria (sulfur limits, API gravity ranges, vapor pressure). Required for cargo acceptance/rejection decisions, dispu
Externally-known business identifier for the term contract. Used in commercial correspondence and legal documentation.

Reference number of the master commercial contract or framework agreement governing this account relationship. Links to the contract management system for term offtake, tolling, or long-term supply agreements. [Moved from account: This attribute currently exists in the account product but represents a master contract reference. In a multi-product supply agreement scenario, each product has its own contract terms. The contract_reference in account may represent a framework agreement, while term_contract captures product-specific contracts. If contract_reference is truly product-specific (not a framework), it should move to term_contract as contract_number.]
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Contract Management; COPAS JOA Standards

description: Current lifecycle status of the term contract. Drives eligibility for order placement and delivery scheduling.
Classification of the commercial contract structure. Aligns with account_classification in the account master.
Committed volume quantity for this product under the term contract. Expressed in the product's standard unit of measure (barrels, cubic meters, metric tons).
System timestamp when this term contract record was created.
Incoterms and delivery obligations specific to this product-account contract. May override account-level default delivery terms.
Calendar date on which this term contract becomes legally effective and delivery obligations commence.
Calendar date on which this term contract expires or terminates. Drives contract renewal workflows.
System timestamp when this term contract record was last updated.
Minimum volume the counterparty is obligated to purchase under take-or-pay provisions. Null if take_or_pay_flag is false.
Fixed differential (premium or discount) applied to the pricing benchmark for this product-account combination. Expressed in currency per unit (e.g., USD/BBL).
Commercial pricing mechanism defining how the contract price is calculated. May reference benchmark indices (WTI, Brent, HH), fixed prices, or formula-based calculations with differentials.
Acceptable quality specification ranges for this product under this contract. Defines tolerances for API gravity, sulfur content, and other product-specific quality parameters.
Contract renewal mechanism defining how the contract may be extended beyond the expiration date.
Indicates whether this contract includes take-or-pay provisions requiring minimum volume offtake regardless of actual delivery.
Unit of measure for contracted volume. Must align with product's standard UOM.

Unique surrogate identifier for the delivery point record. Primary key for the delivery_point data product. Entity role: MASTER_RESOURCE — represents a physical or logical delivery location managed by the business.

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Delivery points (terminals, loading facilities, custody transfer points) incur operating costs (maintenance, personnel, utilities) tracked to cost centers for facility-level cost management and AFE tr

Foreign key linking to compliance.operating_license. Business justification: Delivery points such as marine terminals, pipeline interconnects, and loading facilities require operating licenses from regulatory authorities. Operations teams must verify valid licenses before sche

Foreign key linking to land.operator. Business justification: Delivery points (terminals, custody transfer points, loading facilities) are operated by specific entities responsible for facility operations, safety, and custody transfer. Critical for logistics coo

Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Delivery points (terminals, pipelines) are typically operated by third-party vendors. Business process: tariff management, custody transfer agreements, logistics service procurement, operator performa

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Delivery points generate revenue (throughput fees, tariffs) and costs tracked to profit centers for asset-level profitability analysis and segment reporting. Essential for midstream operations financi

Street address or physical location description of the delivery point facility. Classified as confidential organizational contact data. Used for logistics dispatch, trucking directions, and regulatory permit filings.

Specific berth or jetty number at a port or marine terminal delivery point. Used for vessel scheduling, port authority coordination, and cargo nomination documentation. Applicable to tanker, LNG, and LPG delivery modes.
reference: IMO Maritime Standards; UNECE LOCODE; API RP 1165

description: City or municipality where the delivery point is located. Used for address resolution, logistics routing, and regulatory permit identification.

ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the country in which the delivery point is physically located (e.g., USA, GBR, NLD, SAU). Used for regulatory jurisdiction determination, export/import compliance, and geographic reporting.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; BOEM Offshore Leasing Regulations; EPA Environmental Regulations

description: Timestamp when this delivery point record was first created in the system of record. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and SOX compliance requirements. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: Sarbanes-Oxley Act (SOX); ISO 55001 Asset Management; NIST Cybersecurity Framework

description: Indicates whether this delivery point is a designated custody transfer point where title and measurement responsibility passes between parties. Custody transfer points require API MPMS-compliant metering, sampling, and quality certification. Critical for revenue accounting and joint interest billing (JIB).
reference: API MPMS; COPAS Petroleum Accounting Standards; SAP SD Module

description: Official customs authority port or entry point code assigned to this delivery location for import/export declaration purposes. Required for international crude oil, LNG, and refined product shipments. Aligns with CBP (U.S. Customs and Border Protection) or equivalent national customs authority codes.
reference: U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP); UNECE Trade Facilitation; EPA Export Regulations

description: Date on which this delivery point was or is scheduled to be permanently decommissioned and removed from service. Nullable for active locations. Used for asset lifecycle management, contract termination planning, and regulatory P&A (Plug and Abandonment) reporting.
reference: ISO 55001 Asset Management; BSEE Offshore Safety Regulations; API RP 1165

description: Primary transportation mode used to deliver product at this delivery point (pipeline, tanker, truck, rail, or barge). Determines applicable logistics, tariff, and scheduling rules. Supports multi-modal delivery planning for crude oil, LNG, LPG, NGL, and refined products.
reference: PHMSA Pipeline Safety Regulations; FERC Tariff Regulations; API RP 1165

description: Externally-known, human-readable unique business identifier for the delivery point (e.g., terminal code, rack code, pipeline interconnect ID). Used in nominations, scheduling, and logistics communications with counterparties and operators. Aligns with SAP SD delivery location codes and Avocet Production Operations location identifiers.

Full descriptive name of the delivery point (e.g., 'Houston Ship Channel Terminal', 'Cushing Pipeline Interconnect', 'Rotterdam LNG Discharge Berth 3'). Used in commercial documents, logistics scheduling, and customer-facing communications.

Classification of the delivery point by facility type. Drives logistics scheduling rules, product compatibility checks, and nomination workflows. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: terminal|pipeline_interconnect|rack|refinery_gate|storage_facility|port_berth|fpso_offloading|lng_regasification|rail_siding|truck_loading — promote to reference product]
reference: API RP 1165; PHMSA Pipeline Safety Regulations

description: Date from which this delivery point became or becomes commercially and operationally active. Used for contract validity checks, nomination eligibility, and historical reporting. Aligns with SAP SD validity period management.
reference: SAP SD Module; FERC Tariff Regulations; COPAS Petroleum Accounting Standards

description: Indicates whether this delivery point handles Hydrogen Sulfide (H2S)-bearing streams. H2S service locations require specialized safety equipment, personnel training, and HSE protocols per OSHA 29 CFR 1910.119 Process Safety Management and API RP 55.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.119 Process Safety Management; API RP 55; Enablon HSE Management

description: Date of the most recent regulatory or internal safety inspection conducted at this delivery point. Used for HSE compliance tracking, maintenance scheduling in Maximo Asset Management, and regulatory audit readiness.
reference: PHMSA Pipeline Safety Regulations; BSEE Offshore Safety Regulations; ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety; Maximo Asset Management

description: Geographic latitude coordinate of the delivery point in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum). Used for GIS mapping, offshore/onshore classification, proximity analysis, and logistics route optimization. Supports integration with GIS systems.
reference: WGS84 Geodetic Datum; API RP 1165; BOEM Offshore Leasing Regulations

description: Number of loading or discharge arms available at the delivery point. Determines simultaneous loading capacity and influences scheduling of concurrent cargo operations (SIMOPS). Applicable to marine terminals, rack locations, and pipeline interconnects.
reference: API RP 1165; PHMSA Pipeline Safety Regulations

description: Geographic longitude coordinate of the delivery point in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum). Used for GIS mapping, offshore/onshore classification, proximity analysis, and logistics route optimization.
reference: WGS84 Geodetic Datum; API RP 1165; BOEM Offshore Leasing Regulations

description: Maximum vessel size in Deadweight Tonnes (DWT) that can be accommodated at this marine delivery point. Used for vessel compatibility screening during cargo scheduling and nomination management for crude oil and LNG tanker deliveries.
reference: IMO Maritime Standards; API RP 1165; BSEE Offshore Safety Regulations

description: Identifier of the fiscal or allocation metering system installed at this delivery point, as registered in the OSIsoft PI System or SCADA/DCS. Used to link delivery volumes to measurement data for custody transfer, allocation, and revenue accounting.
reference: API MPMS Chapter 5 (Metering); OSIsoft PI System; FERC Metering Requirements

description: Minimum vessel size in Deadweight Tonnes (DWT) required to safely operate at this marine delivery point. Used alongside max_vessel_dwt for vessel compatibility screening during cargo scheduling.

Scheduled date for the next mandatory regulatory or internal safety inspection at this delivery point. Drives preventive maintenance scheduling in Maximo Asset Management and HSE compliance calendar management.
reference: PHMSA Pipeline Safety Regulations; BSEE Offshore Safety Regulations; ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety; Maximo Asset Management

description: Minimum number of calendar days in advance that a delivery nomination must be submitted for this delivery point. Drives nomination management workflows in Avocet Production Operations and SAP SD scheduling. Ensures operational readiness and regulatory compliance.
reference: FERC Natural Gas Regulations; API RP 1165; Avocet Production Operations

business_glossary_term: Naturally Occurring Radioactive Material (NORM) Risk Flag
description: Indicates whether this delivery point has identified Naturally Occurring Radioactive Material (NORM) risk associated with produced water, scale, or sludge handling. NORM-flagged locations require specialized waste management, personnel monitoring, and regulatory reporting per EPA and state radiation control programs.
reference: EPA NORM Regulations; OSHA Radiation Safety Standards; Enablon HSE Management

description: Indicates whether the delivery point is located offshore (True) or onshore (False). Offshore delivery points are subject to BSEE/BOEM regulatory oversight, additional HSE requirements, and FPSO/marine logistics protocols.
reference: BSEE Offshore Safety Regulations; BOEM Offshore Leasing Regulations; API RP 2SK

description: Description of the delivery point's operational hours (e.g., '24/7', 'Mon-Fri 06:00-22:00 Local', 'Daylight hours only'). Used for logistics scheduling, nomination window management, and customer delivery planning.

Current operational lifecycle state of the delivery point. Controls whether the location is available for nomination and scheduling. 'suspended' indicates temporary unavailability; 'decommissioned' indicates permanent retirement from service.

Name of the pipeline system or network to which this delivery point is connected (e.g., 'Colonial Pipeline', 'Trans-Alaska Pipeline System'). Applicable for pipeline_interconnect and terminal delivery point types. Used for tariff determination and FERC/PHMSA regulatory reporting.
reference: FERC Natural Gas Pipeline Regulations; PHMSA Pipeline Safety Regulations

description: Postal or ZIP code for the delivery point location. Used for logistics routing, tax jurisdiction determination, and regulatory compliance filings.
reference: GDPR Article 4; PHMSA Pipeline Safety Regulations

description: Business-defined geographic region or operating area in which the delivery point is located (e.g., 'Gulf Coast', 'North Sea', 'Middle East', 'Asia Pacific'). Used for regional logistics planning, commercial reporting, and supply chain segmentation.
reference: OPEC Regional Classification; API District Codes

description: Primary regulatory jurisdiction governing this delivery point (e.g., 'FERC', 'BSEE', 'EPA Region 6', 'PHMSA', 'UK HSE'). Determines applicable safety, environmental, and operational compliance requirements. Used for regulatory reporting and permit management.
reference: FERC Natural Gas Regulations; BSEE Offshore Safety Regulations; EPA Environmental Regulations; PHMSA Pipeline Safety Regulations

description: State, province, or administrative subdivision where the delivery point is located. Required for regulatory jurisdiction mapping (e.g., state environmental permits, PHMSA state pipeline safety programs) and tax/royalty reporting.
reference: PHMSA Pipeline Safety Regulations; EPA Environmental Regulations

description: Maximum storage capacity of the delivery point facility expressed in barrels (BBL). Principal quantitative fact for storage facilities, terminals, and FPSOs. Used for inventory planning, nomination management, and capacity allocation. Applicable to crude oil, NGL, LPG, and refined product locations.
reference: API MPMS (Manual of Petroleum Measurement Standards); API RP 1165

description: Applicable pipeline or terminal tariff code governing the transportation or throughput charges at this delivery point. Used for freight cost calculation, FERC tariff compliance, and SAP SD pricing condition determination.
reference: FERC Tariff Regulations; PHMSA Pipeline Safety Regulations; SAP SD Module

business_glossary_term: Throughput Capacity (Barrels of Oil Per Day)
description: Maximum throughput or loading/discharge rate of the delivery point expressed in Barrels of Oil Per Day (BOPD). Used for logistics scheduling, nomination management, and capacity constraint modeling. Applicable to terminals, racks, pipeline interconnects, and refinery gates.
reference: API MPMS; FERC Tariff Regulations; API RP 1165

description: IANA time zone identifier for the delivery point location (e.g., 'America/Chicago', 'Europe/Amsterdam', 'Asia/Riyadh'). Used to correctly interpret operating hours, nomination deadlines, and delivery timestamps in logistics and scheduling systems.
reference: IANA Time Zone Database; FERC Natural Gas Regulations

description: UN/LOCODE five-character code identifying the port, terminal, or logistics location (e.g., USGLS for Galveston, NLRTM for Rotterdam). Mandatory for international trade documentation, LNG/LPG cargo nominations, and customs declarations. Aligns with UNECE Trade Facilitation standards.
reference: UNECE LOCODE; FERC Natural Gas Regulations; IMO Maritime Standards

description: Timestamp when this delivery point record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, data synchronization, and audit compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: Sarbanes-Oxley Act (SOX); NIST Cybersecurity Framework; ISO 55001 Asset Management

description: Water depth in metres at the offshore delivery point location. Applicable only to offshore berths, FPSO offloading points, and subsea pipeline interconnects. Used for vessel compatibility assessment and marine logistics planning.
reference: BOEM Offshore Leasing Regulations; BSEE Offshore Safety Regulations; IMO Maritime Standards

description: Unique system-generated surrogate identifier for the customer delivery nomination record. Primary key for the nomination entity in the Silver Layer lakehouse.

Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: A nomination is a formal customer delivery request — it must be tied to the commercial account making the request. nomination currently has delivery_point_id, petroleum_product_id, and operational fie

Foreign key linking to exploration.block. Business justification: In PSA and concession regimes, crude lifting nominations must reference the producing block for production allocation, government reporting, and royalty calculation. Nomination scheduling and entitlem

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Nomination processing, scheduling coordination, and pipeline/terminal operations costs are allocated to logistics/operations cost centers for operational cost tracking and efficiency analysis.
Reference to the nominated delivery point (terminal, pipeline custody transfer point, or discharge port) where the product is to be received by the customer.
reference: API RP 17V; PHMSA Pipeline Safety Regulations

description: Foreign key linking to venture.joa. Business justification: JOA partners nominate equity crude liftings per JOA lifting procedures and nomination cycles. Required for JOA-compliant nomination workflow, operator approval process, partner lifting coordination, a

Foreign key linking to land.lease. Business justification: Crude oil nominations are tied to specific lease production entitlements based on working interest and NRI. Essential for offtake scheduling, entitlement verification, and ensuring liftings match owne

Foreign key linking to venture.partner. Business justification: Nominations represent partner equity holders requesting their entitlement share under PSA/JOA. Business requires tracking which venture partner is nominating (distinct from the commercial account buye

Reference to the original nomination record from which this amended nomination was derived. Null for original nominations; populated for all amendment versions to maintain the amendment history chain.

Foreign key linking to product.petroleum_product. Business justification: Nominations specify which petroleum product (crude grade, diesel, gasoline, propane, LNG) is being nominated for lifting or delivery. Core supply chain operation requiring product master data for spec

Foreign key linking to venture.psa. Business justification: PSA contractors nominate cost recovery and profit oil liftings per PSA terms. Essential for PSA entitlement lifting execution, contractor nomination procedures, government approval workflow, and fisca

Foreign key linking to procurement.contract. Business justification: Crude oil nominations frequently reference master supply or framework contracts that establish pricing formulas, quality specifications, and delivery terms. Essential for contract compliance verificat

The actual quantity of product loaded or delivered as measured at the custody transfer point, expressed in the same unit of measure as nominated_volume. Populated post-lifting from terminal or metering records.
reference: API MPMS Chapter 12 — Calculation of Petroleum Quantities; PHMSA Measurement Standards

description: The total number of amendments applied to this nomination since original submission. A value of zero indicates the nomination has not been amended. Used to track nomination stability and customer behavior patterns.

Categorized reason code for the most recent amendment to this nomination. Supports analysis of nomination instability drivers and commercial risk patterns. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: volume_change|vessel_substitution|laycan_change|product_change|delivery_point_change|customer_request — promote to reference product]
The date on the bill of lading issued at the load port upon completion of cargo loading. Serves as the pricing date for Dated Brent and other spot-referenced cargoes and triggers revenue recognition.
reference: INCOTERMS 2020; IFRS 15 Revenue from Contracts with Customers; API MPMS Chapter 17

description: Free-text description of the reason for nomination cancellation when nomination_status is 'cancelled'. Captures commercial, operational, or force majeure circumstances leading to cancellation.
reference: Oil Gas Internal Nomination Procedures; COPAS Petroleum Accounting Standards

description: The date and time at which the nomination was formally confirmed by the Oil Gas commercial/trading desk. Marks the transition from submitted to confirmed status and triggers downstream logistics scheduling.
reference: Oil Gas Internal Nomination Procedures; SAP S/4HANA SD Module

description: The date and time at which the nomination record was first created in the system. Audit trail field capturing the initial record creation event for data lineage and compliance purposes.
reference: SOX Section 302/404 — Internal Controls; IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition

description: The INCOTERMS 2020 delivery basis governing risk transfer and cost allocation between Oil Gas and the customer. Determines whether Oil Gas arranges freight (CIF/CFR) or the customer nominates the vessel (FOB). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FOB|CIF|CFR|DES|DAP|FCA|EXW — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: INCOTERMS 2020 — International Chamber of Commerce; COPAS Petroleum Accounting Standards

description: The ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the final destination country for the cargo. Required for export licensing, OFAC/sanctions compliance screening, and country-of-destination reporting to regulatory bodies.
reference: OFAC Sanctions Regulations; U.S. Export Administration Regulations (EAR); BSEE Export Reporting

description: The UN/LOCODE five-character code identifying the port or terminal where the cargo is to be discharged and delivered to the customer. Applicable for CIF, DES, and DAP delivery terms.

The International Maritime Organization (IMO) unique seven-digit vessel identification number prefixed with 'IMO'. Permanent vessel identifier used for vetting, sanctions screening, and port state control compliance.
reference: IMO Resolution A.1078(28) — Ship Identification Number Scheme; SOLAS Chapter XI-1 Regulation 3

description: Indicates whether the customer's KYC documentation has been verified and is current at the time of nomination submission. Supports AML compliance and counterparty due diligence requirements.
reference: FATF AML/CFT Recommendations; SOX Section 302/404; Oil Gas KYC Policy

description: The last date of the laycan window — the latest date by which the vessel must arrive and tender NOR. If the vessel arrives after this date, the charterer has the right to cancel the charter party.
reference: INCOTERMS 2020; Charterparty Standard Terms; BIMCO Voyage Charter Clauses

description: The first date of the laycan (laydays cancelling) window — the earliest date the vessel may arrive at the loading or discharge port and tender Notice of Readiness (NOR) under the charter party terms.
reference: INCOTERMS 2020; Charterparty Standard Terms; BIMCO Voyage Charter Clauses

description: The calendar month (YYYY-MM format) of the monthly lifting program to which this nomination belongs. Used to reconcile nominations against the monthly crude lifting schedule and production allocation.
reference: Avocet Production Operations; Oil Gas Monthly Lifting Program Procedures

description: The UN/LOCODE five-character code identifying the port or terminal from which the cargo is to be loaded onto the vessel. Applicable for FOB and similar delivery terms where Oil Gas is the seller at the load port.
reference: UN/LOCODE Port Code Standard; BIMCO Port Definitions

description: The quantity of product formally requested by the customer in the nomination. Expressed in the unit of measure specified by the volume_uom field (BBL, MT, or MMBTU depending on product type and contract terms).
reference: API MPMS Chapter 12 — Calculation of Petroleum Quantities; SPE PRMS

description: The calendar date on which the customer formally submitted the nomination request. Represents the principal business event date for the nomination lifecycle.
reference: Oil Gas Internal Nomination Procedures; Avocet Production Operations

description: Current workflow lifecycle state of the nomination. Tracks progression from initial customer submission through commercial confirmation, amendment, cancellation, or expiry without fulfillment.
reference: Oil Gas Internal Nomination Procedures; SAP S/4HANA SD Module

description: Classification of the nomination by commercial basis and delivery mode. Distinguishes between term contract liftings, spot sale cargoes, pipeline nominations, and product exchange arrangements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: term_contract|spot_sale|pipeline|cargo|exchange — promote to reference product]
reference: COPAS Petroleum Accounting Standards; FERC Natural Gas Pipeline Regulations

description: For pipeline gas nominations, the NAESB/FERC nomination cycle in which this nomination was submitted (Timely, Evening, Intraday 1, 2, or 3). Null for marine cargo nominations.
reference: NAESB WGQ Business Practice Standards; FERC Order 587 — Gas Pipeline Nomination Standards

description: The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the price differential and any fixed price components are denominated (typically USD for international crude and LNG trades).
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS 9 Financial Instruments

description: The contractual premium or discount (in USD per BBL or USD per MMBTU) applied to the benchmark pricing basis for this specific nomination. Reflects quality, location, and timing adjustments negotiated in the contract.
reference: COPAS Petroleum Accounting Standards; IFRS 9 Financial Instruments

description: The benchmark price index against which the nominated cargo will be priced per the contract terms (e.g., Dated Brent, WTI Cushing, Platts Dubai, NYMEX Natural Gas). Drives price calculation at bill of lading date or delivery. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: dated_brent|wti_cushing|platts_dubai|argus_sour|nymex_ng|ice_brent — promote to reference product]
reference: Platts Price Assessment Methodology; Argus Media Pricing Standards; FERC Market Transparency Rules

description: Externally-known alphanumeric reference number assigned to the nomination, used in communications with the customer, terminal operators, and logistics teams. Follows the NOM- prefix convention.
reference: Oil Gas Internal Nomination Procedures; SAP S/4HANA SD Module

description: The specific delivery date requested by the customer within the laycan window, or the target pipeline delivery date for non-vessel nominations. Used for scheduling and logistics planning.
reference: Oil Gas Internal Nomination Procedures; SAP S/4HANA SD Module

description: Indicates whether the customer, vessel, and destination country have been screened against OFAC, UN, EU, and UK sanctions lists prior to nomination confirmation. Must be True before confirmation_timestamp is populated.
reference: OFAC SDN List Compliance; UN Security Council Sanctions; EU Sanctions Regulations; UK OFSI Sanctions

description: The FERC-assigned or pipeline operator-assigned code identifying the shipper of record for pipeline nominations. Required for FERC-regulated interstate pipeline capacity scheduling and balancing.
reference: FERC Order 636 — Pipeline Restructuring; PHMSA Pipeline Safety Regulations

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this nomination record originated. Supports data lineage tracking in the Silver Layer lakehouse. SAP_SD for SAP Sales and Distribution, AVOCET for Avocet Production Operations, ETRM for Energy Trading and Risk Management system.
reference: Oil Gas Data Governance Policy; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Standards

description: The maximum allowable percentage above the nominated volume that may be delivered without requiring a separate nomination amendment. Expressed as a positive percentage (e.g., 5.00 for 5% over).
reference: INCOTERMS 2020; Standard Crude Oil Sale and Purchase Agreement Terms

description: The minimum allowable percentage below the nominated volume that the customer may accept at delivery without triggering a contract shortfall or take-or-pay obligation. Expressed as a negative percentage (e.g., -5.00 for 5% under).
reference: INCOTERMS 2020; Standard Crude Oil Sale and Purchase Agreement Terms

description: The date and time at which the nomination record was most recently modified. Updated on every status change, amendment, or data correction to support audit trail and change tracking requirements.
reference: SOX Section 302/404 — Internal Controls; Oil Gas Data Governance Policy

description: The name of the tanker or LNG carrier nominated by the customer or charterer to lift the cargo. Populated for marine cargo nominations; null for pipeline nominations.
reference: IMO Ship Identification Standards; BIMCO Vessel Nomination Clauses

description: The outcome of the vessel vetting process conducted against OCIMF SIRE or CDI inspection records. Approved vessels are cleared for loading; rejected vessels must be substituted; conditional approvals require additional review.
reference: OCIMF SIRE Programme; CDI Marine Inspection Standards; BSEE Offshore Safety Regulations

business_glossary_term: Volume in Barrels of Oil Equivalent (BOE)
description: Nominated volume converted to Barrels of Oil Equivalent (BOE) for cross-commodity comparison, portfolio reporting, and SEC reserves disclosure alignment. Conversion factors applied per API/SPE standards.
reference: API MPMS; SPE PRMS Reserves Classification; SEC Regulation S-X Rule 4-10

description: Unit of measure in which the nominated volume is expressed. BBL (barrels) for crude and liquid products, MT (metric tonnes) for LNG and bulk cargoes, MMBTU for natural gas, MCF/MMCF for pipeline gas volumes, M3 for metric volume reporting.
reference: API MPMS Chapter 11 — Physical Properties; FERC Gas Measurement Standards

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the offtake entitlement record. Primary key for this entity in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Entity role: MASTER_AGREEMENT — represents a contractual entitlement container defining a counterparty's right to lift volumes under a term offtake agreement or PSA lifting schedule.

Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: offtake_entitlement defines a customer's entitlement under a term offtake agreement. It already has customer_counterparty_id (→ customer_counterparty) but lacks a direct FK to the commercial account.

Foreign key linking to exploration.block. Business justification: Production entitlement allocation by block is a standard E&P operational and regulatory requirement. Blocks are the primary unit for production sharing and government reporting. An E&P production acco

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Entitlement administration, overlift/underlift reconciliation, and JV partner coordination costs are allocated to commercial/JV administration cost centers for overhead tracking and partner billing.
Reference to the customer or counterparty (IOC, NOC, refiner, trader, or wholesale buyer) holding this offtake entitlement. Identifies the lifting party in the JOA or PSA partner lifting schedule.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module; COPAS JIB Standards

description: Foreign key linking to product.crude_grade. Business justification: PSA/JOA equity crude entitlements specify field-specific crude grades for cost recovery and profit oil calculations. Grade-specific pricing differentials, quality specs, and blending constraints are e

Foreign key linking to customer.delivery_point. Business justification: Offtake entitlement delivery_point string should be a FK reference to the delivery_point master table. This enables proper referential integrity and allows joining to get full delivery point details.

Foreign key linking to exploration.discovery. Business justification: PSA/JOA entitlement allocation reports and commerciality declarations require linking offtake entitlements to the originating discovery. Entitled volumes, equity share, and profit-oil calculations are

Foreign key linking to venture.joa. Business justification: Offtake entitlements derive from JOA working interest and net revenue interest allocations. Critical for JOA-based entitlement calculation, equity production allocation, partner balancing, and overlif

Foreign key linking to exploration.license. Business justification: Offtake entitlements are legally grounded in the exploration/production license. License terms define royalty rates, net revenue interest, and working interest percentages that directly determine enti

Reference to the parent term offtake agreement, Joint Operating Agreement (JOA), or Production Sharing Agreement (PSA) under which this entitlement is granted. Links the entitlement to its governing commercial contract.

Foreign key linking to land.operator. Business justification: Offtake entitlements specify the operator managing production and lifting operations for the entitled volumes. Critical for production accounting, JIB reconciliation, and operational coordination betw

Foreign key linking to product.petroleum_product. Business justification: Offtake entitlements specify which petroleum product the customer is entitled to lift under PSC/JOA agreements. Essential for equity crude allocation, gas balancing, and revenue distribution calculati

Foreign key linking to procurement.contract. Business justification: Offtake entitlements often governed by processing, transportation, or handling service contracts (e.g., tolling agreements, pipeline capacity contracts). Essential for cost allocation, tariff reconcil

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Equity production entitlements (working interest, net revenue interest) are allocated to field-level profit centers for upstream accounting, reserve booking, and field economics. Core upstream financi

Foreign key linking to venture.psa. Business justification: PSA profit oil and cost recovery volumes establish contractor offtake entitlements. Essential for PSA entitlement determination, cost recovery ceiling tracking, profit oil split calculation, and contr

Foreign key linking to reservoir.reserves_estimate. Business justification: Offtake entitlements in PSA/JOA structures must be traceable to certified reserves estimates for SEC/PRMS regulatory reporting. Auditors and reserves certifiers require that entitled volumes are bound

Foreign key linking to reservoir.reservoir. Business justification: In PSC/JOA arrangements with reservoir-specific cost recovery pools (e.g., separate accounting for deepwater vs shallow reservoirs in same field), offtake entitlements are calculated and tracked at re

Authorization for Expenditure (AFE) number associated with the capital or operating program linked to this entitlement period. Used for JIB cost allocation and partner billing reconciliation in SAP Joint Venture Accounting.
reference: SAP Joint Venture Accounting; COPAS Model Form Accounting Procedure; AFE Industry Standard

description: Classification of the governing commercial agreement type under which this entitlement is established. Determines the applicable accounting treatment, partner balancing rules, and regulatory reporting obligations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PSA|JOA|term_offtake|spot|GSA|LNG_SPA|FPSO_offtake — promote to reference product]
reference: SPE-PRMS; COPAS; IFRS 15; SEC Regulation S-X Rule 4-10

description: Method used to resolve overlift/underlift imbalances between JOA partners at period end. Cash settlement involves monetary payment; in-kind involves physical volume make-up; carry-forward defers the imbalance to the next period; deemed-sold treats the imbalance as a notional sale.
reference: COPAS Model Form Accounting Procedure; SAP Joint Venture Accounting; IFRS 15

description: Type of financial security instrument required from the customer to support credit exposure under this entitlement. Determined by credit rating and exposure level. Letter of credit and bank guarantees are standard instruments in international crude oil and LNG trading.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; UCP 600 (ICC); IFRS 9; Basel III

description: Volume of cost oil or cost gas allocated to the contractor party for recovery of capital and operating expenditures under a Production Sharing Agreement (PSA). Expressed in the entitlement unit of measure. Applicable only to PSA-governed entitlements.
reference: PSA Industry Standard; SPE-PRMS; IFRS 6; COPAS

description: Timestamp when this offtake entitlement record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides the audit trail creation anchor for SOX compliance and data lineage tracking.

Maximum credit exposure approved for this customer under this offtake entitlement, expressed in the transaction currency. Enforced during cargo nomination and lifting authorization workflows to manage counterparty credit risk.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; COPAS; IFRS 9 Credit Risk; Basel III

description: External or internal credit rating assigned to the customer counterparty (e.g., S&P: AAA, AA+, BBB-; Moody's: Aaa, Baa1; or internal rating code). Used in credit risk assessment and determination of collateral or letter of credit requirements for lifting authorization.
reference: S&P Global Ratings; Moody's; Fitch; IFRS 9; Basel III; SAP S/4HANA FI

description: Total volume actually lifted by the customer against this entitlement from the period start date through the most recent reconciliation date. Expressed in the entitlement unit of measure. Used to compute remaining entitlement and overlift/underlift position.

ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which monetary values (entitlement value, overlift/underlift value, settlement amounts) are denominated. Typically USD for international crude and LNG transactions.
Mode of transportation used to deliver lifted volumes from the production source to the delivery point. Determines applicable logistics, demurrage, and PHMSA/FERC regulatory requirements.

Total contractual volume the customer is entitled to lift during the entitlement period, expressed in the designated unit of measure (BBL, MT, or MMBTU). Represents the gross entitlement before any adjustments for overlift/underlift carry-forward.
reference: SPE-PRMS; PSA/JOA Industry Standard; COPAS Model Form

description: Externally-known business identifier for this offtake entitlement record, typically assigned by the operator or JV administrator. Used in JIB statements, lifting schedules, and partner correspondence. Aligns with SAP JVA document numbering conventions.
reference: SAP Joint Venture Accounting; COPAS Model Form Accounting Procedure

description: The last calendar date of the entitlement period. Nullable for open-ended or evergreen offtake arrangements. Defines the closing boundary of the lifting window. Used in overlift/underlift position calculations and period-end reconciliation.

The first calendar date of the entitlement period during which the customer is contractually authorized to lift volumes. Defines the opening boundary of the lifting window under the term offtake agreement or PSA schedule.

Current lifecycle state of the offtake entitlement. Drives operational eligibility for scheduling lifts and triggers credit/compliance checks. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|suspended|terminated|pending|expired|cancelled — promote to reference product if additional states are required]

The customer's equity entitlement percentage, which may differ from Working Interest (WI) due to PSA cost recovery provisions, carried interest arrangements, or government back-in rights. Used in PSA lifting schedule calculations to determine the contractor's equity oil/gas share.
reference: PSA Industry Standard; SPE-PRMS; IFRS 6; SEC Regulation S-X Rule 4-10

description: Maximum allowable overlift or underlift volume before formal balancing or cash settlement is triggered. Expressed in the entitlement unit of measure. Contractually defined in the JOA or offtake agreement to accommodate operational variability in cargo sizes.
reference: COPAS Model Form Accounting Procedure; JOA Industry Standard

description: International Commercial Terms (Incoterms 2020) code defining the allocation of costs, risks, and responsibilities between seller and buyer for the delivery of lifted volumes. Governs title transfer, insurance obligations, and freight cost allocation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FOB|CIF|CFR|DES|DAP|FCA|EXW|DDP|CPT|CIP — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: Incoterms 2020 (ICC); COPAS; SAP S/4HANA SD Module

description: Joint Interest Billing (JIB) code used to allocate costs and revenues to the correct partner account in the joint venture accounting system. Enables accurate partner billing and cost recovery tracking in SAP Joint Venture Accounting per COPAS standards.
reference: SAP Joint Venture Accounting; COPAS Model Form Accounting Procedure

description: Indicates whether the customer has completed Know Your Customer (KYC) due diligence verification as required for counterparty onboarding under anti-money laundering (AML) and sanctions compliance programs. Must be True before entitlement activation.
reference: FATF Recommendations; FinCEN AML Rules; OFAC Sanctions; SOX

description: The most recent date on which the cumulative lifted volume, remaining entitlement, and overlift/underlift position were formally reconciled against production allocation records. Used to determine the currency of the entitlement position data.
reference: COPAS; Avocet Production Operations; SAP Joint Venture Accounting

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this offtake entitlement record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change tracking, audit compliance, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.

Classification of the lifting schedule cadence governing when the customer may nominate and execute lifts against this entitlement. Drives operational planning in Avocet Production Operations and cargo scheduling systems.
reference: Avocet Production Operations; JOA/PSA Industry Standard; COPAS

description: The Net Revenue Interest (NRI) percentage representing the customer's share of production revenue after deducting royalties, overriding royalty interests (ORRI), and other burdens. Distinct from WI as it reflects the net economic interest after all encumbrances.
reference: COPAS; Quorum Land System; SEC Regulation S-X Rule 4-10; SPE-PRMS

description: Number of calendar days prior to the scheduled lifting date by which the customer must submit a formal volume nomination. Contractually defined in the offtake agreement and used to trigger operational readiness workflows.
reference: JOA/PSA Industry Standard; Avocet Production Operations

description: Monetary value of the overlift or underlift position, calculated by applying the applicable pricing formula to the overlift/underlift volume. Used for cash settlement or in-kind balancing between JOA partners. Expressed in the transaction currency.
reference: COPAS Model Form Accounting Procedure; SAP Joint Venture Accounting; IFRS 15

description: Net overlift (positive) or underlift (negative) position of the customer relative to their entitlement as of the last reconciliation date. Expressed in the entitlement unit of measure. A positive value indicates the customer has lifted more than their entitlement (overlift); negative indicates underlift. Critical for JOA partner balancing and cash settlement calculations.
reference: COPAS Model Form Accounting Procedure; SAP Joint Venture Accounting; SPE-PRMS

description: Contractual payment terms governing settlement of invoices for lifted volumes (e.g., Net 30 days from Bill of Lading date, prepayment, letter of credit at sight). Defined in the offtake agreement and enforced in SAP S/4HANA SD billing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module; COPAS; IFRS 15; Incoterms 2020

description: Quality or location differential applied to the benchmark price to arrive at the actual transaction price for lifted volumes. Expressed in the transaction currency per unit of measure (e.g., USD/BBL). May be positive (premium) or negative (discount) relative to the benchmark.
reference: API MPMS; COPAS; Platts; Argus Media

description: The benchmark or pricing formula basis used to value lifted volumes under this entitlement (e.g., WTI, Brent, Dubai Fateh, Henry Hub for gas, JKM for LNG). Determines the reference price index applied in overlift/underlift cash settlement calculations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WTI|Brent|Dubai|ANSI|Henry_Hub|JKM|NBP|formula|Platts_dated — promote to reference product]
reference: FERC; Platts; Argus Media; COPAS; IFRS 9

description: Volume of profit oil or profit gas allocated to the contractor party after cost recovery under a Production Sharing Agreement (PSA). Represents the contractor's share of production above the cost recovery tranche, split per the PSA profit-sharing formula.

Residual volume the customer is still entitled to lift within the current entitlement period, calculated as entitled volume minus cumulative lifted volume. Stored as a business-tracked field updated at each reconciliation cycle rather than computed on-the-fly, supporting operational scheduling decisions.

The applicable royalty rate percentage deducted from gross production entitlement before computing the customer's net lifting volume. Includes government royalties, lessor royalties, and overriding royalty interests (ORRI) as applicable under the governing lease or PSA.
reference: BOEM; BSEE; Quorum Land System; COPAS; ONRR Reporting Requirements

description: Indicates whether the customer and associated entities have been screened against applicable sanctions lists (OFAC SDN, EU Consolidated List, UN Sanctions) as part of the counterparty compliance process. Required for regulatory compliance in international crude and LNG trading.
reference: OFAC; EU Sanctions Regulation; UN Security Council Resolutions; SOX

description: Standard unit in which entitlement volumes are expressed and tracked. BBL (barrels) for crude oil and condensate, MT (metric tonnes) for LNG/LPG, MMBTU for gas, MCF/MMCF for gas volumes. Governs all volume calculations within this entitlement record.

The type of legal instrument by which this working interest was created or transferred. Deed = mineral deed, Assignment = assignment of interest, Farmout = farmout agreement, Lease = oil and gas lease, PSA = Production Sharing Agreement, JOA = Joint Operating Agreement.

The condition that triggers conversion of this interest to another type, if applicable. Payout = converts after payout, Time-Based = converts after a specified date, Production-Based = converts after cumulative production threshold, Event-Based = converts upon a specific event.
Indicates whether this interest is convertible to another interest type (e.g., ORRI converts to WI after payout). True = convertible, False = not convertible.

The method by which costs are allocated to this working interest. Proportionate = costs allocated by WI decimal, Non-Consent = non-consenting party penalty provisions apply, Penalty = penalty interest rate applies, Carried = costs borne by another party.

description: The date on which this working interest record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.

The date on which this working interest ownership becomes effective. Used for revenue and cost allocation calculations.
The date on which this working interest ownership expires or terminates, if applicable. Null for indefinite interests.
Business identifier or reference number assigned to this working interest for tracking and reporting purposes. May be system-generated or externally assigned.

Classification of the ownership interest type. WI = Working Interest (cost-bearing), ORRI = Overriding Royalty Interest (non-cost-bearing), NRI = Net Revenue Interest, RI = Royalty Interest, COPAS = COPAS-defined interest category.

The decimal fraction representing the net revenue interest after deducting royalties, overriding royalties, and other burdens from the working interest. Represents the owner's share of revenue. Example: 0.1875 = 18.75% NRI.

description: Indicates whether the interest owner is the designated operator of the lease or well. True = operator, False = non-operator partner.
